observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and Acting City Accountant to review their records to ascertain the whereabouts and status of the unsubmitted DVs totaling P1,652,942.94 and immediately submit the same to the Audit Team, otherwise pertinent disbursements will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The City and Acting City Accountant has exerted efforts to locate some of the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. The City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer immediately submit the same to the Audit Team, otherwise pertinent disbursements will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: There are still nine unsubmitted DVs and locating some of the supporting vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC - General Services Officer (GSO) to:

a. Submit the duly accomplished PIF to the Office of the Supervising Auditor or Audit Team Leader and the GIF of GSIS on or before April 30 of each year to be used as basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage of all the City’s insurable properties; and 

b. Insure with the GIF of GSIS all the City’s insurable assets and properties with the appropriate third party liability, comprehensive insurance coverage to protect the City thru indemnification in cases of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO has submitted the list of insured buildings and vehicles. The OIC-GSO has insured some of the buildings and vehicles covered. The GIF of GSIS and the list of insured buildings and location and other information was not submitted.
observations.reason: Not all the necessary documents to the GIF of GSIS were submitted. Insurance coverage for some of the buildings and vehicles was accomplished but the comprehensive list and supporting information was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Treasurer instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to conduct timely reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments in their books, if warranted, to arrive at a correct cash balance every recording period.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to maintain cashbook for all CIB-LCCA accounts by bank account and by fund which shall be updated and balanced daily.
For the twelve (12) stale checks:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to:
a. Inform the concerned payees about their stale checks and that they could surrender the check for issuance of a new one; and
b. Coordinate with the City Accountant for the reversion of the payables to unappropriated surplus of the general fund or other necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted.
For the reconciling items reflected in the BRS:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to exert utmost effort to identify the details of the reconciling items with a net amount of P44,524,457.31 so that appropriate journal entries could be drawn by the latter in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Cash books were provided for all CIB accounts.
JEV No. 101-24-12-3140 dated March 20, 2024 were drawn to reverse the remaining stale checks.
Upon receipt of the Debit Memo and Credit Memo, JEV no. 101-24-12-3141 dated March 20, 2024 was drawn to adjust the reconciling items in the books.
observations.reason: Several reconciling items remain unrecorded in the books due to the insufficiency of details.

The Inventory Committee is in the process of resolving/consolidating items identified as missing during the inventory taking for disposition. The CGSO, CEO, and CAO have collaborated and committed to implementing measures to complete the one-time cleansing activities. However, time constraints, coordination challenges, and the sunset provision in COA Circular No. 2020-006 affecting derecognition have delayed full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant thoroughly review the long and/or unsubstantiated outstanding payables totaling P8,295,693.53, prepare appropriate adjusting entries to revert them to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund, unless sufficient evidence is submitted to the Audit Team to establish that the payables are founded on valid claims; and inform in writing the concerned suppliers and contractors about their unclaimed retention money with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the City should proper claim be not made within reasonable period.
observations.managementAction: Prepared adjusting entries to revert to the unappropriated surplus the outstanding payables under JEV Nos. 101-24-04-3571 and 401-19-12-617 dated April 1, 2024 and December 27, 2024 respectively, and informed the suppliers and contractors about their unclaimed retention money with advice that the amount will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the City should proper claim be not made within reasonable period.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is still reviewing the details of other long-outstanding payables and is continuously reviewing the details of other unsubstantiated payables that are not yet identified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution and recognize the portion to "Welfare Goods for Distribution" only upon receipt of report/proof of distribution by the CGSO or other concerned departments; and require the City Accountant to maintain SLCs for all welfare goods as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.managementAction: The AOs were directed to properly record the purchases and recognize the portion to "Welfare Goods for Distribution" only upon receipt of report by the receiving departments. The City Accountant was directed to maintain SLCs for all welfare goods.
observations.reason: No management action was taken for the maintenance of SLCs for all welfare goods. The CAO has yet to assign a personnel to prepare and maintain the SLCs for all welfare goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant jointly evaluate the impairment of the receivables and comply with the documentary requirements and guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables amounting to P859,749.99 or to avail of the one-time cleansing of dormant receivables within one year from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Continuing evaluation of the dormant receivables for write-off. Coordination with CTO ongoing to ensure that a request for write-off will be prepared and submitted after thorough review and evaluation of the dormant receivables.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the dormant receivables for write-off is ongoing. The CAO is experiencing difficulties due to lack of supporting documents of the recorded receivables. Nonetheless, CAO in coordination with CTO will prepare request for write-off after thorough review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (CDRRMC) to:

a. Identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the projects and activities to be charged out of the previous years’ unexpended and the continuing appropriations, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

b. Validate whether the PPAs funded by the continuing appropriations for CY 2021 and 2022 will still be implemented; otherwise, transfer the funds to the STF.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the LDRRMFIP did not include the projects and activities to be charged against the LDRRMF. However, in CY 2025, the City already complied with this recommendation.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, the LDRRMFIP still did not include the projects and activities to be charged against the LDRRMF and the unexpended balances of the LDRRMFIP; however in CY 2025, the City complied with this recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The LCE is negotiating with the private investors from the retail industry for possible privatization of market operations to address the issues faced by the Public Market, hence, all the audit recommendations were not yet implemented by the City.
observations.reason: No action was made by the City during the year, citing the planned privatization of the concerned asset or operation as the primary reason for non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Direct the City Accounting Office and OIC-City General Services Officer (GSO) to first update and compare their respective records, that is the latest RPCPPE and the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs), prepare the inventory working papers for submission to the Inventory Committee and the City and the latter, to revisit, re-assess and accordingly adjust the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), before proceeding with the inventory taking of the City; and b. Follow the succeeding procedures for the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office updated first the Semi-Expendable Properties (SEPs) before the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing.
observations.reason: Ongoing conduct of Preliminary Activities by the Offices of the City Accountant and City GSO for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accounting Office and OIC-City GSO to continue with the conduct of Preliminary Activities for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts and to follow COA Circular No. 2020-006 procedures.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office updated first the SEPs before the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing. Continuous reconciliation of PPE accounts is being made.
observations.reason: Ongoing conduct of Preliminary Activities by the City Accounting Office and City GSO; reconciliation and physical inventory activities are reiterated and ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Mayor direct GFPS TWG to ensure all PPAs included in GPB have well-defined gender issues and establish GAD M&E System; convene working group to develop system framework.
observations.managementAction: Working meetings held; GAD M&E Team and training conducted; GFPS created and capacity building conducted. However, shortage of qualified personnel and changes in GAD unit organizational structure affect full implementation.
observations.reason: Changes in organizational structure and shortage of qualified personnel/expertise in gender analysis and GAD M&E delay establishment of robust GAD M&E.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC – General Services Officer (GSO) and City Accounting Office to proceed with the following activities of One- Time Cleansing of PPE using the procedural guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to finally dispose or derecognize the missing PPE amounting to P46,596,823.81 and thereby establish a reliable balance of PPE accounts, to wit: a. Section 7.0 - Disposition for non-existing/missing PPEs; b. Section 8.0 - Derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and c. Section 9.0 - Accounting entries and disclosure in the notes to the Financial Statements. The City should take note of the sunset provision therein emphasizing that the procedures could no longer be used to further derecognize missing properties and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The inventory committee has submitted the request communicating the findings on the initial review of the documents submitted to the Audit Team. The City Mayor issued a memorandum to the City.

Additionally, it was determined in the initial review that the attached investigation reports for non-existing/missing PPEs whose accountability/responsibility could not be pinpointed after the investigation, were not signed by the Investigation Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency.

As of this writing, the response and corrective actions were not yet received by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Letter dated February 3, 2025 communicating the findings on the initial review of the documents submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. Concerned accountable officers to submit to the GSO the accomplished forms as required under Section C of Part II of the Manual on Disposal of Government Property; and b. Disposal committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction:
The City Mayor issued a memorandum to all the concerned offices to submit the accomplished forms required for all the unserviceable properties to the GSO.

The disposal committee facilitated the disposal of the first batch of the unserviceable properties and 36 items amounting to P6,966,026.38 were disposed on March 31, 2024. The second batch of disposal on August 22, 2024 consists of 10 items amounting to P780,300.00.

The GSO has yet to receive the accomplished form required for all the unserviceable properties from the concerned offices.
observations.reason: The GSO has yet to receive the accomplished form required for all the unserviceable properties from the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMC to ensure that significant projects and programs in the LDRRMFIP are implemented to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of the City and its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2024, the City had a total available and utilized LDRRMF of P160,781,529.34 and P58,540,569.11, respectively, broken down as presented below: Current Year's Appropriation: Quick Response Fund (QRF) P19,251,905.96 available, P19,155,420.00 utilized, P96,485.96 balance; Mitigation Fund (M F) MOOE P36,921,113.89 available, P17,364,019.11 utilized, P19,557,094.78 balance; Capital Outlay (CO) P8,000,000.00 available, P180,900.00 utilized, P7,819,100.00 balance. Continuing Appropriation P19,232,467.00 available, 0.00 utilized, 19,232,467.00 balance. Special Trust Fund CY 2021 P2,992,063.50 available, 2,992,063.50 utilized; CY 2022 P34,754,403.20 available, P18,848,166.50 utilized, P15,906,236.70 balance; CY 2023 P39,629,575.79 available, 0.00 utilized, P39,629,575.79 balance.

Total as of December 31, 2024 P160,781,529.34 available, P58,540,569.11 utilized, P102,240,960.23 balance.
observations.reason: The unutilized portion of the LDRRMF mainly consisted of funds allocated for response and rehabilitation during typhoons and other calamities that typically occur in the last quarter of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and City Accounting Office (CAO) to avail of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE using the procedural guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to establish a reliable balance of PPE reported to the statements; a) Section 6.3 - Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; b) Section 7.0 - Disposition for non-existing/missing PPEs; c) Section 8.0 - Derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and d) Section 9.0 - Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements
Also, we recommended that the City take note of the sunset provisions therein emphasizing that the procedures could no longer be used to further derecognize missing properties and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in PPE accounts.
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken.
observations.reason:
The Management has yet to fully reconcile the records of PPE accounts despite availment of One-Time Cleansing. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 62 to 67, Part II of thisReport
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Accountant, in coordination with the City Assessor, the City GSO and other concerned officials, to identify all the parcels of land owned by the City and ensure that all properties owned by the City are accounted in the books; b. City Accountant, in coordination with the City GSO, to analyze and identify to a specific parcel of land the survey cost and other expenses totaling P183,223.65, otherwise make the necessary adjustments by charging these amounts to the Prior Years’ Adjustment account; c. City Accountant to reclassify the road lots totaling P1,745,600.00 from the Land account to the Road Networks account to achieve consistency in the presentation of accounts; d. City Accountant and/or the City GSO to submit to the Audit Team copies of proof of ownership of 11 parcels of lot which were recorded in the books and inventory records; e. City GSO or the concerned offices/assignees to facilitate the registration and transfer of the titles of the parcels of lot/land determined to be owned by the City Government from the previous owner/s to the City government; and f. City Accounting Office to maintain RPLC and the City GSO maintain PCs for all lands owned by the City.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken.
observations.reason:
The CAO will implement the audit recommendation to transfer P1,745,600.00 from the Land account to the Road Networks account. However, the office will review the PPE items of P93,815.00 since they may be purchased as part of a set.
The 47 PPE items without acquisitions costs listed in the Machinery and Equipment, Motor Vehicles, Furniture and Fixturesand Other PPEaccounts will be reviewed as they maybe purchased as part of a set, determining whether these items should be recorded as PPE or Semi-expendableproperties.
The CAO will comply with the requirements of the MNGAS for LGUs by inserting additionalcolumnsfor thecustodyandsourcedocumentstotheexisting SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accounting Office, City GSO and the City Agriculturist to coordinate and come up with an updated report on the status, condition, location, accountability and other relevant information needed for proper recording and/or derecognizing of biological assets in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The CGSO, the Office of the City Agriculturist and the CAO are currently reconciling their records and reports to ensure that the status, condition, location accountability and other relevant information are included for the proper monitoring and documentation of the biological assets of the City Government.
The CAO ensures that biological assets are properly valued in the financial statements at each reporting date.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 5, Page Nos. 71 to 73, Part II of thisReport
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. City Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to record the unrecorded Accounts Payable account of P45,757,044.16, the related PPE accounts of P11,338,659.93, and the CIP account of P34,418,384.23; and b. City Accountant to coordinate with the City Engineering Office for the submission of the Status of Completion of Projects as of year-end to ensure that all unpaid completed and ongoing projects are recorded in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of Accounts Payable and the counterpart asset accounts at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
The CAO requested a copy of the List of Payables for CY 2024 Infrastructure Projects as of December 31, 2024 from the CEO, which only included six CIP accounts out of 23 projects.
The CEO consistently and timely submits Quarterly Reports on Government Programs, Projects and Activities (GPPAs). On February 21, 2025, the CAO requested a copy of the List of Payables for CY 2024 Infrastructure Projects as of December 31, 2024, and the CEO provided it.
observations.reason: As a prior year audit observation, no adjusting entry to reflect the unrecorded Accounts Payable, the related PPE accounts, and the CIP account, was submitted to the Audit Team. Moreover, it was disclosed that completed and ongoing projects that were not recognized as accounts payable as of December 31, 2023 remained unrecorded and were only paid in CY 2024 since they were one-time payment projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned implementing office or department to coordinate with the source agency or if not known, with the DBM, on the proper utilization or liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the trustor, if provided in the terms of the grant and/or seek approval from the trustor to revert the balances of these long outstanding dormant accounts to the City’s unappropriated surplus of the GF.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office (CAO) will take the necessary steps to revert the unexpended balance of the Due to NGAs account totaling P2,600,000.00 to the unappropriated surplus of the GF after conducting proper consultation with concerned implementing offices, or return the amount to the trustor or SA, whichever is appropriate and clearly determinable.
observations.reason:
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 7, Page Nos. 75 to 76, Part II of thisReport
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City GSO to cause the registration of the 11 unregistered motor vehicles and the renewal of registration of the 36 motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with Section 5 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74.
observations.managementAction: Four government vehicles were registered as of November 2024, accompanied by photocopies of LTO official receipts, but the remaining four vehicles and 19 with expired registrations have yet to be registered. The 19 vehicles with expired registration were issued to various home owners' associations in the City's various subdivisions, and as end-users, they must comply with the LTO registration requirements. The concerned offices additionally suggested that the aforementioned motor vehicles be donated to end-users.
observations.reason: The City Government has yet to take appropriate action to register the remaining vehicles; some vehicles were suggested for donation to end-users.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and other concerned officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the City to include in its automated payroll system the payment of the wages of its Job Order workers.
observations.managementAction: The City was able to enroll several JO workers in the City payroll accounts.
observations.reason: At least 1,000 job order workers of the City were not yet enrolled in the automated payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City GSO to cause the registration of the 11 unregistered motor vehicles and the renewal of registration of the 36 motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with Section 5 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74.
observations.managementAction: The City was able to register a total of 20 motor vehicles during the year.
observations.reason: The City Government has yet to register four motor vehicles and the remaining 19 vehicles with expired registrations were issued to different home owners' associations in the City's subdivisions; end-users must comply with LTO registration requirements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years' audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for authority to write off the dormant and/or unliquidated receivables to the COA, following the guidelines and procedures provided by COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. A request for write-off was not submitted to COA yet.
observations.reason: The amount was still in the Due from Officers and Employees account. No request for write-off was submitted yet.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties of the Municipality and submit a Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Commission on Audit (COA) Audit Team on or before January 31 of the following year; b. Property Custodian to maintain Property Cards (PCs) for all properties of the Municipality and reconcile these balances with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) of the Accounting Office and the Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs); and
We also recommended that should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records appeared to be improbable, the Municipality shall consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
The annual physical count of all PPEs was not conducted during the year as the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) was only established on November 2024. hence PCs were not maintained, while updating of RPLCs is still ongoing. The Management has yet to implement the One-TimeCleansing.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 33 to 35, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Treasurer or the designated property custodian to identify all land owned by the Municipality and ensure that all properties owned by the Municipality are properly accounted in the books to avoid discrepancies between the records; b. Municipal Treasurer, or the designated property custodian or the concerned/assigned Office to facilitate the registration and transfer of the titles of the procured parcels of lot/parcels of lot from the previous owner/s to the Municipal government; and c. Municipal Accounting Office to maintain RPLC and the Municipal Treasurer or the designated property custodian to maintain PCs for all lands owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
Parcels of land owned by the Municipality were not fully identified. Registration/renewal of registration of the motor vehicles were not facilitated due to budget constraints.
The MGSO was only established on November 2024, hence PCs were not maintained, while updating of RPLCs is still ongoing.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 33 to 35, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording the procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to expense accounts, but instead course them thru the inventory account in consonance with the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer or the designated Supply/Property Officer, to maintain SLCs and Stock Cards, respectively; c. Municipal Treasurer or the designated Supply/Property Officer, to sign the acceptance portion of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) in the deliveries of the procured goods; d. Municipal Treasurer or the designated Supply/Property Officer, to prepare Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for issuances of supplies/materials and to consolidate the issuances on a weekly basis using the SSMI and furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of issued items; and e. Municipal Treasurer or the designated Supply/Property Officer, to conduct the required semi-annual actual count of inventory items and prepare and submit the RPCI to COA every January 31 and July 31.
observations.managementAction:
No actions taken by the Management.
The MGSO started to prepare RIS and SSMI in December2024.
The MAO prepared SLCs.
observations.reason:
SLCs and SCs were not yet maintained by the Municipality of December 31, 2024.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation Nos. 2 and 3, Page Nos. 35 to 37 and 38 to 40, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other concerned officials to:
a. ensure that PPAs charged against the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready by conducting the extensive planning and coordination with other concerned government agencies prior to its inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure that the projects to be incorporated therein are not among those projects to be implemented by other agencies; and b. ensure the timely implementation and procurement of the projects in accordance with the schedules on the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
Twelve PPAs of continuing appropriations amounting to P3,100,000.00 for CY 2024, nor DF reprogrammed for other PPAs. Timely implementation of PPAs was not implemented accordingly. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 50 to 54, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. BAC to comply with the mode of procurement indicated in the approved APP, otherwise prepare a BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HoPE) why competitive bidding or the original mode of procurement will not be pursued, for his approval, and thereafter reflect these changes in the APP; b. BAC Secretariat to post the Request for Quotation (RFQ), NOA, approved contracts or purchase orders, and the NTP, if necessary, for projects with Approved Budget for the Contract (ABCs) of more than P50,000.00, in the PhilGEPS website, website of the Municipality and conspicuous places within the premises of the Local Government Unit (LGU); and thereafter ensure that such postings are documented and attached to the disbursement vouchers; c. BAC to prepare the RFQ containing all essential information and instructions for the supplier/contractors such as due dates for submission of quotations and documentary requirements, time or duration of delivery, among others, to avoid confusion and misinterpretations; otherwise conduct a pre-bid conference for suppliers/contractors to discuss said information; d. BAC to prepare the BAC Resolution recommending award of the contract to the winning bidder, for the approval of the HOPE before entering into a contact or purchase order; e. BAC to submit to the COA Audit Team, for our validation, the lacking supplier’s requirements such as Mayor’s Permit, PhilGEPS registration number, Omnibus Sworn Statements (OSS) and/or PCAB License, if required, for transactions enumerated in Appendix 1; and thereafter make sure that these requirements are automatically attached to the disbursement vouchers; and f. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the above documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing them for payment, otherwise transactions with incomplete documentations will be suspended and/or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
No actions taken by the Management.
BAC to prepare BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HoPE) for the award of the contract.
BAC to prepare RFQ containing all essential information and instructions for the supplier/contractors.
BAC Secretariat to post RFQs and relevant documents in PhilGEPS.
observations.reason: Justifications for changes in mode of procurement were not provided to the Audit Team. RFQs, NOA, approved contracts and NTP were not posted in PhilGEPS. Lacking supplier documents were not attached to DVs. Payments were processed despite incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. analyze and identify consultants whose services or functions could be performed by existing plantilla personnel to minimize the cost of Consultancy Fees incurred by the Municipality; and ensure that in the hiring of consultants for the completion of a specific PPA, services to be rendered are highly technical or special in nature that require skilled experts in their respective fields to avoid unnecessary expenditures; b. direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete and proper documentations were attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing them for payment; and c. direct other concerned officials to submit a written justification on payments made to consultants on months where the NOA, NTP, and contracts for their services were not yet executed.
observations.managementAction:
No actions taken by the Management.
Management replied that to minimize the cost of Consultancy Fees incurred by the Municipality, the Municipal Mayor to analyze and identify the consultants whose services or functions could be performed by existing plantilla personnel to minimize the cost. Compliance with submission of complete documentary requirements and attachments for claims.
observations.reason: Justification that services and additional level of expertise of the hired consultants are deemed necessary for the Municipality was not submitted for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer or the designated Property Custodian to cause the registration of the five motor vehicles and the renewal of registration of the five other motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with Section 5 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74, and thereafter ensure that all vehicles of the Municipality are insured with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Registration/renewal of registration of the motor vehicles were not facilitated by the Management due to budget constraints. Insurance of vehicles with GSIS not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to furnish the COA Auditors with copies of all POs within five days of their execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, and ensure that POs are properly accomplished containing all the basic data or information required under COA Circular No. 96-010; and b. Agency official/s in charge of accepting deliveries to notify the Auditors of incoming deliveries of goods within 24 hours of the scheduled acceptance so that timely inspections could be conducted by the Auditors, if warranted, and effectively establish the regularity of the deliveries.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
The Office of the Auditor was not notified of the scheduled acceptance. The Auditors were not also notified of the time and date of the deliveries of goods within 24 hours of the scheduled acceptance. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 9, Page Nos. 54 to 56, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant, as the one certifying the completeness and propriety of supporting documents, to submit written explanation on why there are erasures/alterations on the above documents; and thereafter ensure that all attachments to the disbursement vouchers are properly accomplished, without erasures, to avoid doubts on their regularity; and b. Require the Municipal Engineer to make sure that the NOA are properly received by the supplier with dates of receipt written in the document itself.
observations.managementAction: No actions taken by the Management.
observations.reason: DVs and other supporting documents have erasures and alterations for CY 2024. Although duly signed, dates of receipts of supplier in NOA were not properly indicated in several transactions.

observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Municipal Assessor will coordinate with the Provincial Assessor to ensure that the revised property valuations in Barangay Timalan Balsahan are reflected in the assessment roll, which will be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer by December 31. The Municipal Treasurer, on the other hand, responded that they will work with the Municipal Assessor to obtain the latest assessment roll needed for the preparation of certified list of taxpayers, including, but not limited to, amounts due and collectible from individual taxpayer. They will also update their existing list of delinquent taxpayers to include uncollected amounts in the current year. The Municipal Accountant also agreed with the audit observation and to record RPT and SET Receivables based on the assessment roll and certified taxpayer list provided by the Assessor and Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to immediately coordinate with the concerned Supplier and demand the refund of the liquidated damages amounting to P18,378.24. Henceforth, ensure that documents supporting the Disbursement Voucher (DV) are properly evaluated before making any payment to ensure that liquidated damages are properly imposed or deducted therefrom, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the BAC Chairperson submitted written communications, dated April15, May 09 and May 13, 2025, between the Municipality and the concerned supplier relative to the uncollected liquidated damage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor promptly constitute a Disposal Committee and its Secretariat who will facilitate the disposal of the Municipality’s unserviceable properties totaling P28,920,222.92, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised Manual on Disposal of Government Properties, to prevent further deterioration, possible loss and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: The Management responded that the Municipal Government had previously constituted an Inventory and Disposal Committee but some of its members are no longer associated with the Municipality. To address this, they are in the process of issuing a new Executive Order (EO) indicating the new members of disposal committee and dividing the existing Inventory and Disposal Committee. He also mentioned that the Local Government expresses its commitment to willfully and faithfully comply with the provisions and requirements of the DBM-COA JMC No. 2024-1 or the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Require the RCOs to remit their collections and prepare the RCDs on a daily basis to prevent accumulation of unremitted collections in their possession and custody, and possible loss, misuse and/or misappropriation of the daily collections, pursuant to Section 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Deposit all collections intact daily or on the next banking day with the Municipality’s depository bank to ensure that the collections are properly safeguarded against the risk of possible loss or unauthorized use, pursuant to Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that it has been the process for the past years that some of the staff who only help during collection period do not remit daily. There are also some instances where bulk transactions for one taxpayer cannot be processed completely within the day. The Acting Municipal Treasurer also responded that she will require RCOs to remit and process RCD daily and will deposit all collections intact daily or on the next banking day. Auditor’s Rejoinder: Being a task force does not exempt them not to remit the collections daily. Likewise, all collections, regardless of amount, must be processed, received, and issued with a corresponding OR which should then be remitted to the Municipal Treasurer or Liquidating Officer on the same day or actual date of collection/receipt.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete and accurate physical inventory of PPE and prepare the RPCPPE in prescribed format and submit to the COA Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; and
b. Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE. Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records appeared to be improbable, consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the concerned Municipal officials during the year.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 34 to 36, Part II of this Report.
For preparation of the Tracking Sheet (RTS) for the recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Inventory Committee was unable to conduct physical count of PPE in CY 2024 and prepare and not submit the corresponding RPCPPE because the concerned Municipal officials were preparing for transfer to the New Municipal Building.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 34 to 36, Part II of this Report.
For preparation of RTS for the recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and to reclassify as SEP the PPE with acquisition cost of less than P50,000.00 per unit in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the SEP items to appropriate inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the concerned Municipal officials during the year.
Adjusting entries to correct the noted deficiencies were already made in the books of accounts under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 06-05150 and 06-00693, both dated June 30, 2024, in General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF), respectively.
observations.reason: The Municipality had not taken action earlier; various SEP items with total acquisition costs of P747,907.00 were still carried in the books of accounts as PPE as of the year-ended December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to adopt measures to eliminate the cash overdraft by exercising diligence, prudence, and proper control in the Annual Budget and disposition of the Municipality’s funds, limit expenditures within the total income collected and realized during the year, and reduce unnecessary operating expenditures until the cash overdraft is eliminated; b. Direct the Local Finance Committee to analyze trends in tax revenue collections from prior years, determine the amount of income with reasonable assurance of collectability, and use these data as basis or benchmark in preparing the Annual Budget for the ensuing fiscal year; c. Require Municipal Treasurer to intensify the conduct of tax campaign initiatives to boost the collection and promote prompt payment of taxes due to the Municipality; and d. Direct the Local Finance Committee to utilize the revenue realized in excess of the target amounts for the settlement of the prior year’s payables and cash back-up for the fund transfers, trust liabilities and continuing appropriation.
observations.managementAction:
The total obligations incurred by the Municipality for the current year amounting to P222,515,966.43 had not exceeded both the total available appropriations of P263,831,542.92 and total realized revenue and receipts of P235,046,516.66, thus may have contributed in the reduction of the cash overdraft of the Municipality in CY 2024.
The Municipality opened a separate depository account with the Landbank of the Philippines (LBP)-Cavite City Branch exclusively for the 20 percent DF to ensure the availability of funds for such purpose.
Adjusting and other actions were noted in the subsequent audit period.
observations.reason: The Municipality had incurred cash overdraft amounting to P53,472,544.54 in the GF as of the year-ended December 31, 2024, partly due to setting-up of unrealistic revenue projections in the Annual Budget for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Enjoin the Local Finance Committee, Committee on Finance, Budget and Appropriations, Municipal Accountant and other responsible officials to submit to the Audit Team the basis used in the computation of the target collectibles for CY 2023 and the justifications for the failure of the Municipality to realize said targets; b. Direct the Local Finance Committee, Committee on Finance, Budget and Appropriations to submit a specific, measurable, attainable, relevant, and time-bound action plan that will outline their recommended fiscal policies and strategies to address the problem of unrealized income; c. Direct the Committee on Finance, Budget and Appropriations to judiciously and meticulously review the annual budget of the Municipality in order to prevent the inclusion of items of income that are not collectible; and d. Direct the Local Finance Committee to strictly set a more realistic budget based on previous year’s actual performance in terms of revenues and expenses in accordance with the existing guidelines on budget preparation.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee submitted to the Audit Team on June 6, 2024 the basis in the computation of the target collectibles for CY 2023 and the justification for the failure of the Municipality to realize said targets. The Local Finance Committee also set a more realistic budget for subsequent year and provided the required justifications.
observations.reason: Estimated revenue for several income for the current year were significantly greater than the average revenue for the same based on the preceding years’ collections, and the Municipality was unable to realize such target revenues with percentage of collection ranging from 9.14 percent to 74.46 percent only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the full implementation of GAD-related programs and projects and to create and establish the GAD Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) Team and System as required under Items 3.4, 5.1 and 5.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GAD focal persons and concerned officials agreed to prepare and submit the required GAD M&E Team and System documents and to coordinate for the full implementation of the GAD PPAs; initial submissions were scheduled.
observations.reason: Due to the cash overdraft and the Municipality’s inability to realize its target revenues for the year, various GAD-related programs, projects and activities (PPAs) were not implemented during the year and the utilization of the GAD Fund was not maximized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace and determine the details of the Due to BIR account amounting to P822,443.39 and cause its immediate remittance and/or make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts as the case may be. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with BIR Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 by ensuring that the correct amount of taxes being withheld and that these taxes are properly remitted within the prescribed period to prevent the incurrence of surcharges and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant indicated the overdue balance of P1,340,475.69 was already remitted to the BIR on May 8, 2025 and submitted BIR Payment Form and payment confirmation as proof; Municipal Accountant will ensure proper and timely remittance going forward.
observations.reason: No action was taken in CY 2023; the overdue unremitted taxes accumulated resulting in outstanding balance. (Remedial action taken and remittance occurred in May 2025.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned personnel to consider availing the One-Time Cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to address the above-cited deficiencies in the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Preliminary activities for the availment of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE were being conducted.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 35 to 38, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications
observations.reason: The Management has yet to implement the One-Time Cleansing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) and other concerned officials, to identify all the land owned by the Municipality and ensure that all properties owned by the Municipality are accounted in the books;
b. Municipal Accountant and/or the Municipal GSO to submit to the Audit Team copies of proof of ownership of one parcel of lot; and
c. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO, to investigate and/or locate the supporting documents of the portions of lot apparently sold to the Metrobank, and submit these documents to the Audit Team for proper derecognition in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant endorsed the list of donated lots to the Municipal Appraisal Committee.
Deed of sale of one parcel of lot was already submitted by the Management.
The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal GSO and the list of cancelled titles were already in the possession of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Relevant supporting documents, which will be the basis for the proper derecognition of the land in the books, were not yet submitted.

observations.managementAction:
PARs for motor vehicles were updated. Vehicles were transferred to the personnel who are actually using the service vehicles to establish proper accountability; Justification that the need for the assignment of more than one vehicle as it is necessary in the performance of their official functions was submitted.
Motor vehicles were monitored and limited the assignment of motor vehicles to the LGU officials and ensured full adherence to the guidelines on the common use of motor vehicles by other officials and employees in the exercise of their core function; the Municipal GSO and concerned officials monitored and limited the assignment of more than one vehicle.
The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO, computed the amount to be deducted from the Transportation Allowance (TA) of personnel based on the actual days they use government vehicles.
observations.reason: Registration of some dump trucks owned by the Municipality were still not updated. Insurance of the dump trucks was still not secured. Some vehicles were not duly marked for proper identification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw the necessary adjusting entry to record any drawn an adjusting entry to record any unused/undistributed cane sticks and walkers to date in its appropriate inventory account; and
b. Adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of supplies and materials and recognize expense only upon issuances thereof to end-users supported with the submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) from the Municipal General Services Office (GSO).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has drawn an adjusting entry with Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 05-9281 dated May 27, 2024.
observations.reason: Procurement for supplies and materials were still being charged directly to expense account. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 38 to 43, Part II of this Report For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. strictly comply with RA Nos. 8291, 7742 and 7875, as amended by RA No. 9241 by ensuring that the amounts of all loan payments and/or contributions withheld are remitted in full to respective government corporations; and
b. investigate the cause of the negative balances of the Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts to facilitate reconciliation and adjusting entries to correct the SLs with negative balances; and
c. establish an effective control system in the recording and monitoring of the Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts for to ensure that entries made thereon are accurate, complete and reconciled with the source documents and to facilitate timely detection of errors.
observations.managementAction:
The Management monitored and ensured that remittances were made within prescribed period.
The Management has updated the registration of motor vehicles and monitored the recording and monitoring of Inter-Agency Payable accounts.
The Management ensured the proper recording and monitoring of Inter-Agency Payable accounts.
observations.reason: Negative balances reflected in Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth accounts were not yet reconciled as these were recorded in the books from prior years.

observations.managementAction:
The Management revised the draft policies/guidelines and plans to public officials and employees were drafted and for approval by the local sanggunian. The Management checked and monitored the mobile phones/plans to avoid excessive expenditures and submit written justifications on the availment of mobile phones in addition to the mobile plan subscription charged to the Municipality.
The Management checked and analyzed the need to provide mobile phones to Officials already granted mobile plan subscriptions and ensured that only qualified personnel were provided with mobile phone/plan.
Partial issuance of PAR and ICS for the mobile phones included in the plan subscriptions of Officials.
observations.reason: The Management revised the draft policies/guidelines which is for evaluation and approval by the local sanggunian. Partial issuance of PAR and ICS for the mobile phones included in the plan subscriptions of Officials. The Management has yet to fully issue PAR and ICS to concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolutions, Municipal DRRM Officer (MDRRMO) and other concerned officials to ensure that all changes in the Annual Budget and AIP be implemented in accordance with Article 417 and 454(b) of the IRR of RA No. 7160, otherwise, affected transactions may be disallowed in audit; and
b. MDRRMO to ensure the implementation of projects, programs, and activities for CY 2024 allocated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to enhance the disaster capabilities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Changes in the PPAs in the AIP for the LDRRMF are supported by SB resolutions.
MDRRMO ensured the implementation of the projects and compliance given.
observations.reason: For implementation of some PPAs, actions are scheduled and ongoing; not all PPAs were implemented by year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC Secretariat to prepare the indicative APP for the ensuing budget year using the APP form approved by the GPPB, which will be submitted to the BAC for the final recommendation of the appropriate procurement modality, and finally to the Head of Procuring Entity (HoPE) for his approval; and
b. BAC to submit their APP and PMR to the GPPB as required in the said Circular and furnish the Office of the Auditor of a copy of each report with proof of submission to the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: The submitted PMR was not in accordance with the prescribed format. No action was taken for proof of submission of APPs to the GPPB.
observations.reason: Proof of submission of PMRs to the GPPB was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal GSO to furnish the COA Auditors with copies of all contracts and POs with supporting documents within five working days from their execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, and ensure that POs are properly accomplished containing all the basic data or information required under COA Circular No. 96-010. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officer/s responsible for accepting deliveries to notify the Auditors of incoming deliveries of goods within 24 hours from the scheduled acceptance.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices assured compliance with the audit recommendation and committed that all POs will be properly filled out and submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period and promised to notify the Office of incoming deliveries of goods within 24 hours of their acceptance.
observations.reason: POs not submitted to COA within five working days from their execution.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team;
b. General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PC based on the RPCPPE and PPELC for each group of PPEs, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation and verification of the year-end balances; and
c. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE.
Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records appeared to be improbable, consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We further recommended:
a. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 and prepare the journal entry and voucher to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below P50,000.00; and the General Services Office (GSO) to follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 on the preparation of ICS for semi-expendable property acquired in CY 2022 onwards and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable Property (RPCSP).
b. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries for the unrecorded depreciation expenses of PPEs using the straight-line method, depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular No. 2017-004, and submit a copy of the journal entry voucher to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
No action has been taken by the Management. Due to manpower constraints, the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office were unable to commence the physical inventory of PPEs.
No action has been taken by the Management. Similar challenges of having limited staff resulted in the inability of the respective offices to maintain the PCs and PPELCs.
No action has been taken by the Management. The reconciliation process is not possible due to the inability of the responsible offices and departments to maintain the PCs and PPELCs, as well as the GSO's failure to prepare the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Due to manpower constraints, the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office were unable to commence the physical inventory of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties of the
City, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year;
b. Property Custodian to maintain property cards based on the RPCPPE; and
c. Property Custodian and OIC-City Accountant to reconcile their respective
property records to determine the reconciled balances of PPE. If the
reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records seems improbable,
reconsider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Assessor to furnish, on or before the 31st day of December every year, the
Acting City Treasurer with a copy of the assessment roll based on the latest
Revised Schedule of Fair Market Values and Property Classification and when
there are changes in the assessed values of real properties due to
reassessments or new assessments to facilitate the preparation of the
CLRPTDC;
b. Acting City Treasurer to prepare and submit, at the beginning of the year, to the
OIC-City Accountant a complete and updated certified list showing the names
of taxpayers along with the amount of taxes due and collectible based on the
RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards. Furthermore, the OIC-City
Accountant is directed to use the list as a basis for setting up the receivables at
the start of the year to facilitate the recording of the actual real property tax
receivables in the books of accounts; and
c. OIC-City Accountant to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of
each year based on the CLRPTDC to ensure the accuracy of these receivable
accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC-City Accountant and the GSO/Property Custodian to maintain SLCs and
SCs for each commodity/stock, respectively;
b. OIC-City Accountant to adjust the books to reflect the correct balance of the
Inventories account based on the actual physical count of the remaining
inventories under the custody of the Supply/Inventory Custodians;
c. GSO to consolidate the RIS on a weekly basis using the SSMI and furnish the
OIC-City Accountant with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of
issued items;
d. OIC-City Accountant, GSO, and Supply/Inventory Custodians to reconcile the
records in the Books with the physical count of inventory at year-end; and
e. GSO and Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Auditor,
using the prescribed format, by July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Acting City Treasurer to prepare a
complete Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year and submit it to the
OIC-Office of the City Accountant. Based on this, the JEV shall be prepared for the
restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and the recognition of the
appropriate payable/liability accounts at the end of the year. In addition, reverse
the entries made in the ensuing year to fairly present the balances of the affected
accounts in the Statement of Financial Position (SFPos).

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to:
a. Intensify efforts to locate relevant records pertaining to the investments;
b. Communicate with the respective agencies regarding the status of the
investments and inquire about the process for updating those investments; and
c. Adjust the books based on the results of the communication for a fair
presentation in the SFPos.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant and other
concerned officials to review and verify records pertaining to outstanding balances
of Due to GSIS, Due to Pagtutulungan sa Kinabukasan: Ikaw, Bangko, Industriya at
Gob yerno (Pag-IBIG) and Due to PhilHealth from prior years in the amount of
P54,422.99, P59,193.63 and P110,107.40, respectively. Thereafter, remit
immediately all insurance premiums and loan payments withheld due to the GSIS,
HDMF and Philhealth as of December 31, 2024 to avoid the incurrence of penalties
and surcharges due to late remittances.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-Accountant to remit all
withheld taxes within the prescribed period to avoid any liability equivalent to the
surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR against the latter and other responsible
officials in their personal capacity.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to prepare
correcting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts
by debiting PPE and crediting AP accounts by P1,624,517.04.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CPDO and OIC-City Engineer to take
immediate remedial actions to ensure that the amount of P5,118,453.04 already
disbursed by the City is not wasted, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be
issued accordingly.
We also recommended that the OIC-City Accountant verify the completion date of
the project named Improvement of MedCare at Barangay 39, Caridad, Cavite City and
deduct any liquidation damages from the final payment, if applicable.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to:
a. Ensure that deliveries of goods are recorded in inventory account upon
procurement and in the corresponding expense upon issuance, transfers, or
write-downs; and
b. Coordinate with the concerned Inventory Custodians to determine the correct
remaining balance of Welfare Goods for Distribution, adjust the account
accordingly, to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant, Human
Resource Management Officer, and other concerned officials to:
a. Submit immediately to the Audit Team the ToR, justification from end-users’
offices, proof of validation by the BAC on their capability to undertake the
consultancy works, and appropriate BAC recommendations to the City Mayor;
b. Ensure that the ToR of individual consultants whose works are either highly
technical or proprietary or primarily confidential or policy determining, where
trust and confidence are the primary consideration, and the justification for their
hiring are prepared and submitted by the end-user units before procurement or
negotiation; and
c. Ensure that validation of consultants’ legal, technical and financial capability and
pertinent recommendation of award have been made by the BAC, pursuant to the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to refrain
from authorizing the release of additional CAs to the AO unless the previous CAs
are fully liquidated or a proper accounting thereof is made as provided in Section
89 of PD No. 1445 and Item No. 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant and all
concerned officials to avoid granting combined CAs to just one personnel and
instead grant CAs to each personnel attending the seminar for proper
accountability establishment and monitoring.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to record
the grant of cash advances in accordance with RCALGU.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to correct the recording of the collected idle tax to reconcile the balances between the RPT and SET Receivables accounts, together with their corresponding reciprocal accounts;

b. City Accountant and the City Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net deficiency of P1,935,532,387.40 between the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and the CLRPTD as of December 31, 2024, and to make the necessary adjustments in their records; and

c. City Accountant to set up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of each year based on the CLRPTD, and to reconcile any discrepancies with the CLRPTD to ensure the accuracy of these receivable accounts at year-end.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant, in coordination with the City General Services Office (CGSO), to coordinate with the concerned Barangays to formalize the transfer of these assets and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for their derecognition from the City's books; and

b. City Legal Office to prepare a Deed of Donation or a Certificate of Turnover and Acceptance for the subject properties and ensure that these documents are executed for similar transactions in the future.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a. City General Services Officer to prepare and submit the RPCI using the prescribed format and instructions provided under Section 56, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;

b. City Accountant to maintain complete SLCs for each inventory item; and

c. City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to facilitate the reconciliation of their respective records and make appropriate adjustments, as necessary, to reflect the reconciled and accurate balances of the Inventory accounts at year-end.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to consistently prepare an adjusting journal entry at year-end to restore/revert the amount of unclaimed checks to the Cash in Bank accounts and recognize the corresponding payables/liabilities to present the correct balance of the accounts, in compliance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and other concerned officials to facilitate the immediate submission of the necessary documents for evaluation by the Audit Team, thereby avoiding audit suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. Henceforth, ensure that the attachment of the requisite documents to the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) is faithfully observed for the purchase of land to attest to the validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to identify other responsible officers and employees, and to prepare a JEV to record in the book of accounts the total amount of P13,000,695.53 under the Due from Officers and Employees account. Furthermore, demand the return of the said amount to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance (ND) by the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to coordinate and facilitate the immediate return of the unexpended and unused fund transfers totaling P9,931,057.77 to the respective SAs or to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure that payments are made directly to the payee/supplier through the issuance of checks, except for those expressly allowed to be paid through CAs under the aforementioned Circular, in order to safeguard government funds from loss or misuse, and to ensure that appropriate taxes are withheld by the City as the withholding agent of the government.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to discontinue the practice of disbursing all payments related to the travel expenses of the City’s officials and employees through a single representative. Instead, individual CAs to be granted for future official travels to ensure responsibility and accountability in compliance with Item Nos. 4.1.5 and 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant to revert the funds to the LDRRMF, subject to compliance with budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations; and

b. City Budget Officer and the City DRRM Officer to ensure that operating expenses are properly charged to the respective GF-MOOE appropriations of the concerned office and that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk reduction and management should be charged to the LDRRMF, in compliance with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the
JEV for the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and
recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts and in addition, reverse
the entries made in the ensuing year, for proper presentation of accounts in the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting Office and the City
Treasurer’s Office to ensure that reconciling items are identified, reviewed, and
promptly addressed to ensure the accuracy of the Financial Statements and
maintain transparency and completeness in financial reporting especially at year-
end.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Accountant to:
• Ensure that uncollected electricity expenses are properly recognized in the
Other Receivables account;
• Conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Receivable balances, to
ensure proper recognition and correct posting of transactions; and
• Perform an aging analysis of all unpaid rentals and electricity expenses,
and if the collectability of long-outstanding amounts becomes doubtful,
provide an allowance for impairment; and
b. City Treasurer and Market Supervisor to:
• Exhaust all remedies to collect the long-outstanding unpaid stall rentals
and electricity expenses; and
• Implement or enforce the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 15-06,
including penalties such as surcharges, cancellation/revocation of
permits/licenses, and/or privilege to occupy or lease any stalls in the public
market to all delinquent stallholders.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. Correctly classify all cash advances in the appropriate Advances accounts
based on their purpose as defined in the RCALGU;
b. Ensure that all the necessary supporting documents, such as the estimates of
expenses for the activity and payrolls, among others, were attached to the DV at
the time of the grant, before processing them for payment; and
c. Submit to the COA Audit Team, in coordination with the concerned
personnel, the lacking supporting documents related to the payments for the
monthly operation and maintenance of Himlayan – General Trias totaling
P1,986,100.00, such as contracts, daily time records, accomplishment reports,
or any other document that establishes the basis and/or validity of the claims to
avoid the issuance of a Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Human Resource and Management
Officer and other concerned officials to consider full transition to the automated
payroll system for processing all employee wages, including those for JO, COS, LSB
teachers, and ALS aides, among others.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City General Services Officer or other authorized agency personnel to indicate the
date of receipt of goods on the duly accomplished DR submitted by the supplier, and
to properly accomplish the AIR indicating dates of inspection and acceptance of the
goods delivered; and
b. City Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete and proper
before recording payables, including duly accomplished DRs, AIRs, and Deed of
Sale (DOS), to establish the proper cut-off date for the City’s liability.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
to:
a. Submit to the COA Audit Team the duly approved time extension with written
notice from the supplier, if any, to avoid imposition of liquidated damages; and
b. Ensure that time extensions, if any, are properly supported with complete
documentation, and strictly impose and deduct liquidated damages equivalent to the
incurred delays of the suppliers before final payment is made.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. Analyze and categorize the asset, liability, income and expenses relative to the
operations of the City Public Market to the special account in the General Fund (GF);
and
b. Prepare separate trial balance and financial statements for the City Public Market,
as an economic enterprise, to be included as part of the GF schedules to assist
concerned City Officials and other stakeholders in their decision-making.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer:
a. Or any concerned officials to submit to the Commission on Audit (COA) Audit
Team the suspension orders duly approved by the City Mayor for the projects
incurring delays due to issues with lot/site locations;
b. And other concerned officials to make recommendations and proposals to
resolve issues ensuring the completion of the projects; and
c. To conscientiously carry out detailed engineering and planning activities, and
issue a certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have
been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and
specifications, before procuring and awarding contracts for infrastructure
projects, to avoid delays and/or suspension of future projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor, through the Human Resource and Management
Officer (HRMO), consider hiring and/or promoting qualified personnel to fill the vacant
plantilla positions to maximize the utilization of the City’s appropriation for PS,
provide additional and secure job opportunities to the public and its constituents, and
reduce the need to hire JO workers.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City LDRRM Officer and City Budget
Officer to refrain from obligating and charging from the LDRRMF for expenditures that
