are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities, in
compliance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC Secretariat or City General
Services Office (CGSO) to furnish the COA Auditors with copies of all contracts and
POs with supporting documents within five days from their execution pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-001, and ensure that POs are properly accomplished containing all
the basic data or information required under COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Direct the City Accounting Office and OIC-City General Services Officer (CGSO) to continue with the conduct of Preliminary Activities on the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts, for submission to the City Inventory Committee as basis of the preparation of Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and the conduct of Inventory Taking; and henceforth
b. Follow the succeeding procedures for the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE accounts provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to properly address the discrepancies in the PPE records.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the total depreciated value of the project “Conversion of Open Space into Parks designated for Senior Citizens” as PPE under the Parks, Plazas and Monuments account.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to check and ensure the completeness and accuracy of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks by verifying the same with the List of Outstanding Checks per Bank Reconciliation Statements and Check Registers to facilitate the preparation by City Accountant of the adjusting entries for the restoration of all the unreleased checks to their respective Cash in Bank and liability accounts, and ensure the fairness of presentation of these accounts in the SFPos.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Appraisal Committee to fast track the assessment of the fair market value of the donated land covered by Deed of Donation dated June 20, 2023 which served as the project site of the infrastructure project “Conversion of Open Space into Parks designated for Senior Citizens at Barangay Toclong I-A, Imus City, Cavite”, and to submit the Appraisal Report to the City Accounting Office for the recording of the donated property.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the Land and Building of the City with carrying amount totaling P5,649,579.07 which were earning rental income, and the accumulated progress payments for the Construction of Four-Storey Building (Rental Housing Units) at Barangay Malagasang I-F, Imus City, totaling P47,927,727.72 as Investment Property considering that these properties have met its definition under IPSAS 16.

Henceforth, require the City Accounting Office to adopt IPSAS 16 and to use the appropriate Investment Property accounts in the RCALGU in the recording of all the properties which meets the definition and examples of Investment Property Assets to ensure the fair presentation of the SFPos of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Require the City Accountant to record all the future purchases of SEPs as inventory under the appropriate SEP accounts and the subsequent issuances thereof to the end-users as expenses under the appropriate SEP Expenses accounts prescribed in Annex B of the COA Circular No. 2024-006 to ensure the fair presentation of the FS of the City; and
b. Require the OIC-City GSO to consolidate all the ICSs issued and prepare the RSPIs at least weekly for submission to the City Accounting Office to serve as the latter’s basis in the recording of the issued SEPs as expenses and likewise, to facilitate the monitoring of all the ICSs issued to ensure that the accountability of concerned employees is verified.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and OIC-City Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to conduct further investigation and reconciliation with the GSIS to validate the existence of the negative balances totaling P3,045,838.04 in the Due to GSIS-Life and Retirement, and State Insurance, and to perform the necessary measures to address the same, including the preparation of the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Due to GSIS and other affected accounts, and coordinating with the GSIS.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) to:

a. Follow-up with the Meralco and if necessary, to submit a formal request to expedite the City’s applications for electric service connection for the four completed facilities/buildings totaling P43,585,452.49 funded out of the 20 percent Development Fund (DF) for them to be usable as soon as possible; and
b. Incorporate in the contract works of future development and other infrastructure projects of the City the application for electric service connection to ensure that all completed projects that will be turned-over by the contractors to the City are capable of immediate use.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the HRMO to prioritize the filling-up of the 10 vacant supervisory and high ranking plantilla positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to maximize the City’s effectiveness and efficiency in delivering public services, and provide additional job opportunities to the public/constituents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the ONI-Chief of Ospital to require the ONI-Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections intact and daily or on the next banking day with the City’s depository bank to ensure that the daily collections are properly safeguarded against the risk of loss or unauthorized use, and to prevent the accumulation of the undeposited collections in her possession/custody to an amount which already exceeds the maximum cash accountability covered by her approved fidelity bond.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the ONI-Chief of Ospital to require the ONI-Cashier Division to avoid the practice of manually altering the ORs if valid mistakes were committed in their preparation. Instead, resort to cancellation of the same and for transparency purposes, the reason/s of the cancellation should be disclosed at the back of the ORs or thru a separate document attached to the ORs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to consistently
prepare an adjusting journal entry at year-end to restore/revert the amount of all
unclaimed checks to the Cash in Bank accounts and recognize the corresponding
payables/liabilities to present the correct balance of the accounts, in compliance with the
above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to use in their procurements the prescribed
PBDs, and other standard forms issued by the GPPB; and
b. BAC to conduct Pre-Procurement Conferences and prepare the corresponding
Minutes of Pre-Procurement Conferences for all procurements exceeding the
mandated threshold as required under the pertinent provisions of Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure that
further disbursement of funds related to the project are net of liquidated damages,
as warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Budget Officer and GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson to ensure that GAD Fund are utilized
solely for programs, projects and activity (PPA) that is responsive to the identified
gender issues, concerns and GAD mandates pursuant to the provisions of PCW,
NEDA and DBM JC No. 2012-01.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant in partnership with the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to
further analyze and trace records pertaining to the Due to GSIS balance
withheld in prior years totaling P2,017,423.80. Thereafter, remit immediately the
withheld amounts due to GSIS in accordance with the IRR of RA No. 8291; and
b. City Accountant in coordination with the City Human Resource Management
Officer and AAO to ensure that amounts withheld and remitted for each of the
24 individuals are accurate, and make representation with the GSIS to address
the inconsistencies in loan amortizations of the subject employees to help them
rectify the situation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the
BRS and submit copies, with all of the necessary supporting documents, to the
Audit Team for verification, within 10 days after the end of each month, as well as
to draw JEVs to take up all valid reconciling items requiring adjustments and
corrections in the books in their corresponding months, to ensure the reliability
andaccuracyof theCash inBankaccounts.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor directthe:
1. OIC-CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records of PPE and
ensure that differences between the RPCPPE, and the PPE balances
presentedintheSFPos and accountingrecordsbe resolved;
2. OIC-CGSO and the City Accountant, in collaboration with the OIC-City
Assessor’s Office, to identify all the parcels of land owned by the City,
including the determination of the specific lots with which the survey costs
were associated, obtaining copies of proofs of ownership of the
aforementioned 11 lots, facilitating the registration and transfer of the land
titles of the acquired lots from the previous owners to the City Government,
and ensuring that these properties are properly accounted for in the books
andtheinventoryreports;
3. City Accountant to reclassify the road lots totaling P1,745,600.00 from the
Land account to the Road Networks account, and the PPE items totaling
P93,815.00tothesemi-expendablepropertiesaccount;
4. OIC-CGSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, to determine the
acquisition costs of the 47 PPE items enumerated in the Machinery and
Equipment, Motor Vehicles, Furniture and Fixtures and Other PPE accounts;
and
5. City Accountant to ensure that the PPELCs and RPLC are prepared, which
include relevant details about the PPE, such as the custody and/or end-user
of the PPE and the reference pertaining to the source documents of the
recordedPPEas of year-end.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor directthe:
a. OIC-CGSO to prepare stock cards of all inventory accounts for proper
monitoring of receipts, issuance and balances of inventory items, and to
conduct the physical count of inventory items, prepare and submit the RPCI
to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
forthefirst andsecond semesters,respectively; and
b. OIC-CGSO and City Accountant to reconcile their Inventory records to
establish the correct and reconciled balances of inventory accounts to be
presentedinthefinancialstatements.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor to:
a. Instruct the City Accountant in coordination with the CEO, to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the unrecorded Accounts Payable, the
related PPE accounts, and the CIP account of P45,841,038.60, P20,181,455.93
and P25,659,582.67, respectively; and
b. Require the CEO to submit to the CAO the Status of Completion of
Infrastructure Projects as of year-end to ensure that all unpaid completed and
ongoing infrastructure projects are recorded in the books of accounts to
reflect the correct balances of Accounts Payable and the counterpart asset
accountsat year-end.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor directthe:
a. OIC-City General Services Officer, City Agriculturist and the City Accountant
reconcile their records and reports, and ensure that the status, condition,
location, accountability and/or other relevant information needed are included
in such reports for proper recording, monitoring and subsequent
derecognition of biological assets in the books of accounts, as necessary;
and
b. City Accountant to adopt the guidelines in recording the Biological Assets as
provided in Paragraphs 16 and 30 of the IPSAS 27 to ensure the proper
valuationof thebiologicalassetsat each reportingdate.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant, in
coordination with the concerned Offices, to formally inform the claimants of their
unclaimed money, with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the
unappropriated surplus of the GF of the City in case no proper claim was made
within a specified period and consequently, cause the immediate reversion of all
dormant payable accountsaged morethantwoyears and/or undocumented tothe
unappropriatedsurplusof theGF.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned implementing offices
to trace and communicate with the SA to locate the MOA and adhere to the
provision therein relative to the utilization and liquidation of the fund transferred,
and/or seek approval from the trustor to revert the balances of these long
outstandingdormantaccountstotheunappropriatedsurplusof theGFof theCity.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City LDRRM Officer and the
Acting CityBudgetOfficer to:
a. Submit a resolution or valid justification explaining the reason/s of charging
the aforementioned expenses to LDRRMF, otherwise, the expenses shall be
disallowedinaudit; and
b. Refrain from obligating and charging to LDRRMF expenditures which are not
directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities
consistentwiththeprovisionsof NDRRMC, DBM and DILGJMCNo. 2013-1.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor directthe:
a. City Engineer to properly and closely monitor the progress of the City’s
implementation of infrastructure projects, issue appropriate notices of
negative slippage requiring the contractors to submit necessary detailed
“catch-up” program and exert efforts to ensure the timely completion of the
implemented projects and to ensure compliance with GPPB Circular No. 13-
2019tofacilitate theeffective management of thecontractors’ performance;
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to ensure the bidders’
performance in ongoing government and private contracts before projects are
awarded to avoid delays in the implementation of the City’s infrastructure
projects; and
c. City Accountant to continue withholding retention money until the Work is
satisfactorilydone andonschedule.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to ensure that the
request for extension were duly supported with valid documentation and the
reasons for contract time extensions are within the conditions enumerated under
AnnexE of the2016Revised IRRof R.A. No. 9184.

Werecommended thattheCityMayor directthe:
a. OIC-CGSO and the City Accountant to prepare the duly accomplished IIRUP,
including all government properties subject for disposal, accompanied by
relevant documents pertaining to the unserviceable properties, as the case
may be, such as individual survey reports, lists of missing spare parts,
stencils of chassis and engine numbers of motor vehicles, and current
photographsshowingallsidesof theproperties, asof December 31, 2024, and
submit the same to the Disposal Committee for inspection, appraisal and
immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent further
deteriorationandreductionof economic value; and
b. OIC-CGSO to gather all unserviceable vehicles and other properties in one
designated location and adequately safeguard the area to avoid additional
damage or loss of the vehicle or its significant parts, that could further
deteriorateits value.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-CGSO to cause the
immediate registration of the remaining four unregistered motor vehicles and the
renewal of registration of the 19 motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with
Section5 ofB.P. Bilang74.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CHRMO, in coordination with
theDepartmentHeads of theCity, to:
a. Ensure that the contracts of the JO clearly provide their specific job
descriptions, duties and expected deliverables against their performance and
accomplishments, for the review and evaluation as to the necessity of their
hiringandregularity ofpayments tothem; and
b. Study the City's manpower needs and hire JO workers based on specific
requests from concerned Department Heads or officials who shall certify that
his or her office needs the services of a JO worker for an identified piece of
work.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City LDRRM Officer to submit the
prescribed monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the
COA Auditor, as required by 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, for proper
monitoringandevaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to conduct re-
assessment of gender mainstreaming by engaging in the activities outlined in
Item 3, Section D of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as well as take into
considerationthe results of program andproject evaluationinterms of benefitsto
target beneficiaries, as assessed by the M&E Team, to ensure the gender-
responsiveness of theGAD PPAs in addressingthe City’s gender issuesand GAD
mandates.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the schedule of all
unreleased checks at the end of the year for proper adjustments in the books;

b. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the Payee for the existence of the check,
one month before becoming stale;

c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the restoration of
cash equivalent to the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate
payable/liability accounts, and in addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year,
for proper presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements (FS); and

d. Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV for the cancellation of the 13 stale checks
totaling P374,803.07, and analyze why these checks were not yet encashed by the
payees, for proper action and ensure proper presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal GSO to reconstruct, prepare and maintain the SPC, RegSPI and RSPI for all
SEP of the Municipality for both previous and succeeding years;

b. Municipal GSO to prepare and submit, at least weekly, the RSPI together with the ICS,
to the Municipal Accounting Office, for all issuances of SEP to the AOs for that period
and the latter to use it as the basis for the recording of SEP expenses accounts;

c. Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain SPLC for all SEP; and

d. Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary journal entries to recognize the
SEP expense amounting to P36,500.00, supported with the required documents from
the Municipal GSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical count of all properties of the
Municipality as a basis for the preparation of the RPCPPE to be submitted to the Auditor
not later than January 31 of each year; and

b. Municipal GSO to prepare and maintain the property cards of all PPE of the Municipality
to be reconciled with the PPE ledger cards maintained by the Municipal Accounting
Office.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider availing the One-Time
Cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, to address the
perennial issues in the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
MENRO to coordinate and prepare an updated report on the status, condition, location,
accountability and any other relevant information necessary for the proper recording
and/or derecognition of the Biological Assets in the books of accounts, in order to arrive
at the accurate balance for presentation in the financial records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office to analyze and identify the variance between the SLs and
the Accounts Payable account presented in the FS;

b. Municipal Accounting Office to trace the unidentified beginning balances from the
previous years to determine if these amounts are still valid, otherwise, drop these
amounts from the books to reflect the correct payables in the FS; and

c. Municipal GSO or other authorized agency personnel to indicate the date of receipt of
goods on the duly accomplished DR to be submitted by the supplier, and to properly
accomplish the AIR indicating dates of inspection and acceptance of the goods
delivered.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct
the overstatement in the Trust Liability – DRRMF account amounting to P11,882,137.71;

b. Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
DRRM Officer to analyze the DRRMF and establish the correct amount of DRRMF
available including STF and continuing appropriation, and thereafter reflect the
established amounts in the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM; and

c. Municipal Accountant to maintain separate SLs of the unexpended balances of
LDRRMF by year of transfer to facilitate easier monitoring of their balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

7.1 Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify the proceeds from the sale of bidding
documents to the Trust Liability account instead of the Guaranty/Security Deposits
Payable account for proper presentation of accounts in the FS;

7.2 Municipal Treasurer’s Office to coordinate with the Land Bank of the Philippines
(LBP) regarding the amount credited to the depository account of the Municipality
amounting to P1,799,476.37, and thereafter, the Municipal Accounting Office prepare
a JEV to adjust the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and the other affected
accounts in the books accordingly;

7.3 Municipal Accounting Office to trace the unidentified beginning balances in the
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account pertaining to the retention money amount
payable to suppliers/contractors totaling P767,619.79, to determine if these amounts are
still valid, otherwise, drop these amounts from the books to reflect the correct payables
in the FS; and

7.4 Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), to identify the reasons why the retention money amounting to P1,633,924.60
remained unreleased to the suppliers, given that some have been outstanding for seven
years, and thereafter, adjust the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and the
other affected accounts in the books accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. MTO and the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct monthly reconciliations between
their records to ensure that cash balances are updated and accurate at the end of each
period;

b. MTO to maintain cashbooks for all bank accounts of the Municipality to be used as
basis of the Municipal Accounting Office for their preparation of the monthly bank
reconciliation statements; and

c. MTO to ensure that cashbooks are ruled, footed, and certified at the end of each
month, in compliance with Sections 26 and 27 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. MEEDMO to submit their monthly monitoring reports of uncollected/unpaid
accounts from their operations to the Municipal Accounting Office to be used as basis of
recording the books to ensure that amounts presented in the FS are reconciled every
reporting period;

b. MEEDMO to adjust and update their monitoring reports on GMA Garden of
Memories to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded and come up with a
reliable and accurate reports;

c. MEEDMO and other concerned Offices to take proactive measures to collect the
outstanding receivables such as sending demand letters or formal notices to delinquent
stallholders that payments are overdue and legal action may be pursued, if necessary;
and

d. Municipal Accountant to provide allowance for impairment on expected losses from
uncollectible or long overdue receivables, if warranted, pursuant to Paragraph 67 of
IPSAS 29.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal GSO to maintain RPLC and Property
Cards, respectively, for each item of land owned by the Municipality for proper
identification and allocation of related costs and expenses;

b. Municipal Accounting Office, upon receipt of copies the necessary documents related
to the two donated parcels of lot, to record the same to the Land account using the fair
market value to ensure proper presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements; and

c. Concerned/assigned Office to facilitate the registration and transfer of the titles of
the parcels of lot/land determined to be owned by the Municipal Government from the
previous owner/s to the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Require the MGSO to update the records of the unserviceable MVs to include the
four unserviceable MVs found during inspection to accurately reflect the details and
numbers of the unserviceable MVs to date;

b. Constitute a Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the
unserviceable properties, particularly the 35 unserviceable MVs already identified by
the Municipality, in accordance with the disposal activities/process enumerated in the
Revised MDGP, to wit:
• Determination of Properties for Disposal
• Submission of Documents Pertaining to Properties for Disposal
• Inspection
• Condition Factor and Rating of the Properties
• Appraisal
• Modes of Disposal
• Dropping from the Books of Accounts

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Office to
coordinate with the relevant implementing office to determine the appropriate action
regarding the dormant funds, whether these amounts will be returned to the SA/trustor or
be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the GF.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical count of all
propertiesof theMunicipality;

b. MGSO to maintain property cards as the basis and reference in the conduct of
physical count andsubsequent preparationof theRPCPPE;

c. Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for each class of PPEs, including
the description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal and
other information about the PPE, for the verification of the year-end balances
and to facilitate the reconciliation of any discrepancy between the balances
per books andtheRPCPPE; and

d. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective PPE records to
come up with the reconciled balances of PPE. Should reconciliation of the
RPCPPE and accounting records appeared to be improbable, consider
availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006datedJanuary31, 2020.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to refrain from directly recording the procurement or
receipt of supplies and materials to expense accounts, but instead course
them through the inventory account in consonance with the Perpetual
InventorySystem ofaccounting; and

b. MGSO to maintain RIS for the issuance of supplies/materials, to consolidate
the issuances on a weekly basis using the SSMI and to furnish the Municipal
Accountant with copies thereof as basis for the recording of the cost of
issueditemsinthebooks.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their Inventory records to
establish the correct and reconciled balances of inventory accounts to be
presentedinthefinancialstatements; and

b. MGSO to prepare stock cards of all inventory accounts for proper monitoring
of receipts, issuance and balances of inventory items, and to conduct the
physical count of inventory items, prepare and submit the RPCI to the Auditor
concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and
secondsemesters,respectively.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. MGSO to conduct verification or physical count of unissued semi-expendable
properties to determine the current status/condition, existence and
serviceability of tangible items with costs of less than P50,000.00, and
whether they were already issued or still in the custody of the MGSO, using
thePARs/AREs asbases;

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries for the
reclassification of the Semi-Expendable Properties recorded as PPE to the
Government Equityaccount; and

c. Municipal Accountant and MGSO to prepare and maintain the relevant reports
for semi-expendable properties as specified in COA Circular No. 2022-004
datedMay31, 2022.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the
Provincial Government to determine the origin and details of the reported
discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts Due from LGUs and Due to
LGUs, and make necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of both
reciprocal accountsarereconciled;and

b. Municipal Accountant to furnish the Provincial Accountant with copies of the
weekly summary of the JEVs showing the breakdown of amounts collected by
the Municipality due to the Provincial Government, as well as copies of the
monthly Abstract of the RPT to facilitate reconciliation with the weekly
summaryof JEVs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
establish and periodically update SLs for all accounts to maintain accurate
balances and transaction monitoring. Moreover, consider computerizing the
accounting system to reduce the time-consuming work required for manual
accountingandtoachieve moreaccurate andreliableaccounting reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the BRS and submit copies, with all of the necessary supporting
documents, to the Audit Team for verification within 10 days after the end of each
monthtoensurethereliabilityandaccuracy ofthe CashinBankaccounts.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to
identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the prescribed format of
the LDRRMFIP, the specific projects and activities to be charged against the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF, along with the continuing
appropriations;

b. MDRRMC to ensure that supplemental budgets or any revisions in the
LDRRMFbudget aresupportedby revisedLDRRMFIP approved by theSB and
the Municipal Mayor, which should then be the basis for the release and
utilizationof theLDRRMFfor theidentifiednewexpenditureitems; and

c. MDRRMC to reprogram continuing appropriations to PPAs that are already
included in the LDRRMFIP, and the revised LDRRMFIP be prepared and
submitted, accompanied by copies of the corresponding approved MDRRMC
and SB resolutions relative to therealignments and/orreprogrammingof the
LDRRMF.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of all POs within five
days of their execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, and ensure
that POs are properly accomplished and contain all the basic data or
informationrequiredunder COACircular No. 96-010; and

b. Agency official/s in charge of accepting deliveries to notify the Audit Team of
incoming deliveries of goods within 24 hours of the scheduled acceptance so
that timely inspections could be conducted by the Auditors, if warranted, and
effectively establishtheregularityof thedeliveries.

Werecommended thattheMunicipalMayor direct the:

a. BAC to ensure that the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184
regarding the Committee’s functions are properly undertaken as adherence to the
principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy of the
Municipality’sprocurement process; and

b. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials and agency
personnel to facilitate the immediate submission to the COA of the lacking
documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted to avoid audit suspension
and/or disallowance. Henceforth, ensure that all required supporting
documents are attached to the DVs before payments to establish the validity
and proprietyof thedisbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
conduct re-assessment of gender mainstreaming by engaging in the activities
outlined in Item 3, Section D of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as well
as ensuring that the GAD M&E Team monitor and evaluate the results of GAD
PPAs in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries, to ensure the gender-
responsiveness of the GAD PPAs in addressing the Municipality’s gender issues
andGAD mandates.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to analyze trends in tax revenue collections
from prior years, determine the amount of income with reasonable assurance of
collectability, and use this data as a basis or benchmark in preparing the Annual
Budget for the ensuing fiscal year;
b. Require Municipal Treasurer, and Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO) to
intensify the conduct tax campaign initiatives to boost the collection and promote
prompt payment of taxes due to the Municipality;
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Daily Cash Position Report and
monitor the availability of cash to prioritize expenditures to avoid the incurrence of
overdraft; and
d. Direct the Local Finance Committee to adopt measures to eliminate the cash
overdraft by exercising diligence, prudence, and proper control in the Annual
Budget and disposition of the Municipality’s funds, and limit expenditures within
the total income collected and realized during the year and reduce unnecessary
operating expenditures until the cash overdraft is eliminated.

We recommended that the Mayor:
a. Require the Supply Office and OIC- Accountant to investigate, identify and reconcile
their respective records to come up with the reliable and correct balances of the
Municipality’s PPE accounts; and
b. Initiate the availment of the Municipality of the one-time cleansing of PPE balances
by following the guidelines and procedures on the six activities prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2020-006 enumerated below:
i. Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking;
ii. Inventory taking;
iii. Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting
records;
iv. Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs;
v. Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available
record of accountability; and
vi. Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant draw
the necessary JEV to correct the balances of the affected accounts by debiting
Construction Materials for Distribution account and crediting Road Networks account
by P28,462,476.00, and also debit for Prior Period Errors and credit Accumulated
Depreciation-Road Networks by P112,663.97.

We also recommended that Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to
prepare the Inspection and Acceptance Report for procured Solar Powered Streetlights
and submit the same to the OIC-Municipal Accountant for recording in the appropriate
ledger cards.

Henceforth, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant exercise caution in
classifying and recording transactions in order to avoid such deficiency, pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with
the Provincial Government to determine the origin and details of the reported
discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts Due from LGUs in the books of the
Municipality and Due to LGUs in the books of the Provincial Government, and make
necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of both reciprocal accounts are
reconciled; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in coordination with the Provincial
Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the AOs to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing and granting additional
cash advance/s to AOs unless their previous cash advances have been fully
accounted for or liquidated as provided in Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and issue the
required certification before granting any cash advance in compliance with Item
No. 1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to investigate the long outstanding
unliquidated cash advances and make appropriate actions based on the results of
the investigation;
d. Instruct the OIC Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances in excess of
their respective maximum cash accountability; and
e. Require the Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of fidelity bond of the
accountable officers to cover the amount of their respective cash advances to
protect the interest of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang
Bayan, formulate and implement clear guidelines, policies, and procedures on the
granting of donations and financial assistance, consistent with the provisions set forth
by the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) MC No. 16 Series of
2022 as amended by DSWD MC No. 06, Series of 2023. These should include
eligibility criteria, documentation requirements, allowable amounts, and an
objective evaluation process to ensure consistency, transparency, and accountability in
the disbursement of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the OIC- Accountant to make representation with various utility companies
for the resolution in their respective bill to allow the withholding of taxes as
required by regulation; and
b. Henceforth, instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper taxes are
withheld before payment of transactions and subsequently remit the same to the
BIR, in accordance with RMO No. 23-2014 and RR Nos. 11-2018 and 13-2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to reconcile all year-end
balances and identify any further inaccuracies in the withheld amounts and
remittances;
b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strengthen the review and validation
procedures for the preparation of the alphalist and related tax reports to ensure the
accuracy and completeness of data used in computing taxes due to the BIR; and
c. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to adopt measures or
procedures to ensure that withholding and remittance balances reconcile, thereby
preventing similar incidents of over-remittance or under-remittance, as the case may
be.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. enjoin the GAD Focal Point System and Municipal Planning and Development
Office (MPDO) to establish the Municipality’s GAD database containing gender
statistics and sex-disaggregated data that have been systematically gathered,
regularly updated, and subjected to gender analysis in the identification, design,
implementation, management and monitoring of the various GAD activities in its
GPB so as to ensure that any gender issues prevailing over its jurisdiction would
be addressed properly and accordingly for the benefits of its stakeholders;
b. GAD Focal Point System ensure compliance with the cited JMC on utilization of the
allocation requirement for GAD Budget of the Municipality; and
c. GAD Focal Point System regularly monitor and ensure the efficient implementation
of GAD PPAs in accordance with DILG endorsed GPB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
transfer the total CY 2024 unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund
(QRF) and the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund-Maintenance and Other
Operating Expenses (DRRMF-MOOE) to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust
Liability as required by the regulation.

Henceforth, we recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to
regularly monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF and ensure that all the unexpended
balance be transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the records
pertaining to the PPE items recorded in lump-sum or without details pertaining
to a specific PPE item totaling P277,597,341.10 and update the PPELCs;
b. Require Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all existing PPE items
owned by the Municipality were physically counted and included in the RPCPPE
and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of respective
records to reconcile the discrepancy amounting to P269,599,936.27 of PPE
balances recorded in the GL and RPCPPE;
c. Initiate the availment of One-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address the recurring issue on
discrepancies of recorded balances of PPE as compared to existing PPE items;
and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to validate and reclassify the: a) duplicate
entry made for the transfer from the TF to the GF of the cost of one unit of motor
vehicle amounting to P2,495,000.00 and the corresponding over-recorded
depreciation expense amounting to P197,520.83; b) mobile trailer used as PNP
Outpost costing P199,000.00 classified as motor vehicle instead of Other PPE;
and c) cost of major improvement of KABAKA covered court amounting to
P991,325.20 recorded as RME instead of recognizing as PPE and the
corresponding unrecorded depreciation expense computed at P132,176.69. Once
validated, prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and submit the
same to the Audit Team for review and validation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipality’s Appraisal Committee
to determine the fair market values of the two portions of Donated Land, and record
the same in the accounting books;
b. Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team a
copy of Bureau of Internal revenue (BIR) Form No. 2000-OT - DST
Declaration/Return (One-Time Transactions) for the payment of DST, if the same
was already paid. Otherwise, immediately coordinate with the BIR for the payment
of the same; and
c. Municipal Assessor and other concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team a
status report on the transfers of Certificates of Title for the two portions of Donated
Land together with copies of documents that will support the accuracy of the
information presented thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and
investigate the cause/s of the discrepancy of P6,586,329.25 between the balances
of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and the List of Unpaid Taxes as of
December 31, 2024. Thereafter, prepare a summary of all the noted discrepancies
and, if warranted, prepare the necessary adjustments in their respective records;
b. Municipal Accountant to validate the noted deficiencies in the entries made to
record the distribution of discounts on advance payments and receipt of municipal
and/or barangay share from RPT/SET collected by PTO in CY 2024. Once validated,
prepare the necessary adjusting entry and submit a copy of the same to the Audit
Team for review and validation; and
c. Municipal Assessor to review and update the existing record or masterlist of all
properties in the Municipality and submit a justification on why the properties
covered by the above-listed 35 TDs were not included in the List of Properties or
Assessment Roll forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer that was used as basis of
recording a set-up or accrual of RPT and SET Receivables for the CY 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Validate the accuracy of noted improper recording of payments for PPE and
rentals of chairs and tables amounting to P154,095.00 and P1,500.00,
respectively;
b. Coordinate with GSO to validate if the total amount of P1,974,435.00 recorded
as Semi-expendable properties were actually issued to all end-users by
requiring the concerned Municipal GSO personnel to submit the properly
numbered and duly accomplished ICS;
c. Once validated, make the necessary adjusting entry for the afore-mentioned
deficiencies and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and duly
accomplished ICS to the Audit Team for review and validation; and
d. Prepare the SPLCs for all purchased Semi-expendables which should
include among others, the impairment and cost of repair, if any.

2. General Services Officer to:

a. Prepare ICS for all Semi-expendable Property issued in CY 2024, in two
copies, one for master file and monitoring purposes and one for submission
to the Municipal Accountant to support the JEV to be made by the latter to
record the issuance thereof; and
b. Prepare and maintain SPCs for each Semi-expendable Property and RegSPI
for each class of issued semi-expendable properties in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006 to ensure
proper monitoring of all issued Semi-expendable Properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to submit the certificates of completion and SWAs to the
Municipal Accountant for the unpaid costs of four completed infrastructure
projects totaling P2,105,905.90 for recording of AP and CIP, thereafter, the transfer
of CIP to related PPE accounts. Henceforth, ensure that all documents pertaining
to completed projects with unpaid amount be forwarded to the Municipal
Accountant at year-end for proper recording/recognition in the books; and

b. Municipal Accountant, after evaluation of the documents to be submitted by the
Municipal Engineer, recognize/record in the books the unpaid costs of the already
completed projects, reclassify the total costs of completed projects from CIP to
appropriate PPE accounts, record the corresponding depreciation expense for the
CY 2024 and submit copies of duly signed Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to the
Audit Team for review and evaluation. Prospectively, always coordinate with the
Municipal Engineer for the status of projects recorded under CIP, especially during
year-end, in order to take up the unpaid costs of completed projects, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Ensure that all supporting documents are in her possession and properly
evaluated for validity of the claim, particularly if goods/services are actually
delivered/rendered, before recording these transactions as AP; and

b. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the documents pertaining to the four
dormant accounts recorded in the AP of the SEF totaling P242,715.00, and if there
were demand for payment from the contractor. Otherwise, cause the immediate
reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus of the GF in compliance with
Section 98 of PD No. 1445, and submit a copy of duly signed Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) to the Audit Team for review and validation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Validate the noted discrepancy of P5,140.18 between the recorded inventory
balances in the SFPos and submitted RPCIs as of December 31, 2024. If
warranted, prepare the necessary adjusting entry and submit a duly signed
copy of the JEV to the Audit Team for review and validation;
b. Require the concerned personnel of the GSO and other concerned departments
to prepare and submit duly accomplished SSMI supported with RIS and/or
distribution lists in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I, and used the same in recording of expenses in the books of
accounts;
c. Fully adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method by recording all the acquisitions of
supplies and materials, except those purchased out of the PCF, to respective
Inventory account; and
d. Prepare and maintain SLCs for each type of supplies and materials for the
proper recording of the receipts and issuances of the same and monitoring of
their balances by the Accounting Office;

2. Require the concerned personnel of the GSO and other concerned departments
to:

a. Prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the SSMI together with the original
copy of the RIS and/or distribution lists to enable the latter to properly record the
issuances of supplies and materials in the books of accounts;
b. Prepare and maintain SCs for each type of supplies and materials for the proper
recording of the receipts and issuances of the same and monitoring of their
balances by the General Services Office; and
c. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all the
supplies and materials every semester and to submit the corresponding RPCIs
to the Accounting Office and GSO not later than July 31 and January 31 of each
year for the first and second semesters, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation
of their respective records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor and other
concerned municipal officials to:

a. Expedite the registration of the titles on the five fully paid parcels of land totaling
P17,008,350.00 under the name of the Municipality by taking necessary actions,
including legal actions to reconstitute the titles of the two parcels of land,
to protect its interests in these subject properties from potential third-party claims;
b. Allocate the necessary budget to start the above actions in transferring the Title
under the name of the Municipality; and
c. Immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking proofs of payment of CGT and
DST for the land acquisitions totaling P5,786,500.00, and a status report on the
registration and/or transfers of corresponding Certificates of Title for review and
validation. Otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Municipal Engineer and the
concerned Contractors to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking
documentary requirements for the subject payments totaling P24,453,057.62 for
further evaluation, otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in audit; and

b. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the documentary
requirements for payments of billings for infrastructure projects prescribed by the
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 are complete before processing
any claims to avoid suspension or disallowance of the transaction in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. MTO and the Municipal Accounting Office to identify the
causes of discrepancies between their records totaling P176,313.43 as of year-end and
prepare the necessary adjustments to come up with reconciled balances;

b. Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that reconciling items are identified,
reviewed, and promptly addressed to ensure the accuracy of the Cash in Bank
account balance and maintain transparency and completeness in financial
reporting especially at year-end;

c. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payee/s of the check/s one
month before they become stale, and prepare the JEV for the cancellation of
the stale checks; and

d. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the schedule
of all unreleased checks at the end of the year for preparation of JEV to restore
the cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate
payable/liability accounts, and in addition, reverse the entries made in the
ensuing year, for proper presentation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, at the earliest and practicable period, require
the Inventory Committee to initiate the One-Time Cleansing of PPE in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006, to address the discrepancies in the PPE accounts
to come up with a balance that is correct and verifiable as to existence, condition
and accountability.

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal GSO to reclassify the properties below P50,000.00 as
SEPs, and prepare and maintain the necessary forms, registry and reports to
strengthen control over the SEPs enumerated in COA Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant:

a. To comply with the procedures for the proper disposition of dormant accounts
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2023-008 to facilitate the derecognition of
dormant accounts amounting to P1,489,948.63, if applicable; and henceforth,

b. To observe the monitoring and reporting requirements of Item 10.0 of COA
Circular No. 2023-008, to wit:
• Prepare and submit the quarterly SDA to the ATL on or before the 15th day of
the ensuing month.
• Ensure that current transactions and those below ten years are properly
documented and appropriately acted upon immediately to avoid being
dormant. If the Accountable Officer (AO) fail to settle his/her accountabilities
despite the demand, the action could either be salary deduction or filing of
appropriate cases
• Disclose comparative information in the Notes to FS relative to the above
dormant accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to reconcile their respective
records of MVs, and to ensure that detailed description—such as plate
numbers or conduction sticker numbers, color, brand—are included to
facilitate the reconciliation process;

b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to identify the six unrecorded
MVs found at station during inspection and, if confirmed to be unrecorded, to
update their respective records accordingly, if warranted; and

c. Municipal GSO to ensure that all government-owned MVs are duly registered
with the LTO and insured under the GSIS in compliance with Section 5 of Batas
Pambansa Bilang 74.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough review of the recorded Receivable
accounts to verify that they represent valid claims of the Municipality, by
securing and maintaining supporting documentation that establishes
evidence of overpayments, to facilitate future recovery efforts;

b. Municipal Accountant and all concerned offices to strengthen internal control
procedures and ensure that all disbursements undergo thorough verification
before processing them for payment by improving coordination and
establishing clear protocols for validating payables to prevent duplication or
erroneous payments; and

c. Municipal Accounting Office and all other concerned personnel to expedite
recovery efforts for the outstanding overpayments and maintain a tracking
system to monitor refund status and detect discrepancies in a timely manner,
otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be issued accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the
COA Audit Team the approved Variation Order (VO), Change Order (CO) or Extra
Work Order (EWO) with complete justifications and necessary approvals, for the
additional road concreting works undertaken in the Public Cemetery, otherwise,
the transaction will be suspended accordingly; and

b. Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future
additional works are properly documented in accordance with the requirements
of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and that strict compliance is observed with the
provisions of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 or Section 71.2.1 of the
IRR of RA No. 12009 (The New Government Procurement Act), in order to
prevent similar audit issues in the future.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents for the CA amounting
to P299,068.00, and thereafter ensure all the necessary supporting documents
were complete in the LRs before recoding the liquidation; and

b. Ensure proper classification of CAs and use the correct accounts in recording the
grant of CAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Offices, to
formally establish and document clear guidelines and criteria for the grant of
financial assistance including, eligibility requirements, prescribed amounts, require
supporting documents, to be strictly followed to ensure consistency and
transparency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. BAC to:
• Ensure that the bidding documents are prepared which contain the
Instruction to Bidders, Bid Data Sheet, GCC and the SCC, among others,
to clearly and adequately define the obligations, duties and/or functions of
the winning Bidder, in accordance with the Philippine Bidding Documents
issued by the GPPB;
• Ensure that the submitted Required Forms of the contractor contain the
mandatory provisions stated in Item 6.0 of GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 and
disqualify non-complying bidders;
• Conduct and/or prepare bid evaluation report, using the pass/fail criterion,
in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 or the RA No. 12009;
• Ensure that all stages of bidding process are properly documented, which
includes, among others, the minutes of opening of bids, abstract of bids as
read, BAC resolutions, NOA and NTP;
• Ensure that NOA, NTP and approved contracts are posted in the PhilGEPS
website within the prescribed dates.

b. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to submit the required
bidding and other supporting documents to the COA Audit Team, and
thereafter, ensure that all progress and final payments to suppliers and
contractors are supported with complete documentation, to avoid suspension
in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering
or the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), to identify the accounts recorded in
the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and the reasons why these
amounts remained unreleased to the suppliers; and

b. Municipal Accounting Office to determine the validity of the recorded claims,
and for accounts without valid claims regardless of age, adjust the
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and the other affected accounts
in the books accordingly, by reverting them in the unappropriated surplus of
the GF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office to maintain separate SLs of the unexpended
balances of Local DDRMF by year of transfer to facilitate the monitoring of their
balances;

b. Municipal Accounting Office to submit to the COA Audit Team the copy of the
monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month; and

c. Municipal Accounting Office and the concerned personnel to:
• provide explanation for the payment of van rentals totaling P100,000.00
made through reimbursement, and submit complete supporting
documents, including contracts or service agreements;
• submit all applicable requirements as prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2012-001 for the payment of honoraria, including basis for the rates
granted; and
• ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the DVs and
refrain the use of RER as a proof of payment for transactions exceeding
P1,000.00 or those that can be purchased from/rendered by
establishments issuing receipts/invoice.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. MHRMO to standardize payroll disbursement procedures and require all JO
personnel to enroll in the automated payroll system, and ensure that any
exceptions must be justified and approved by appropriate authorities with
adequate supporting documentation to ensure accountability and transparency; and

b. MHRMO to require the submission of individual accomplishment reports for
each JO personnel, clearly detailing the nature, scope, and duration of tasks
performed which should be specific, verifiable, and aligned with their job
assignments before processing their wages for payment.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Office and
all concerned Offices to submit valid, BIR-compliant supporting documents or proof
of payment for transactions listed in Annex G, to avoid suspension in audit;
henceforth, strengthen internal control measures to ensure that only fully supported
transactions are processed and paid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Determine the cause/s of the noted discrepancy of PPE balances between the GL
and PPELCs and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that both records are
reconciled. Once reconciled, immediately coordinate with the GSO for the
reconciliation of their respective records in order to proceed with the availment of
the one-time cleansing of PPE to address the recurring issues on discrepancies
between Accounting and GSO records;

b. Submit the justification, details and valid supporting documents for the
derecognized PPE items with total carrying value of P190,104.55 to the Audit Team
for review and validation. Otherwise, pass a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to re-store
the same to the respective PPE accounts and submit the duly signed JEV to the
Audit Team. Henceforth, ensure that all transactions and/or adjustments are based
on verified information and valid documents before recording the same in the
books of accounts; and

the current Plantilla of Personnel, and identify the manpower gaps for

make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the said
accounts; and

b. Constantly review and reconcile the transactions involving reciprocal accounts
so that variances resulting from errors/lapses in recording, if any, can be
immediately adjusted/corrected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) together with other
concerned Municipality Officials to submit a satisfactory explanation to the
Audit Team for evaluation, why the interest expenses and other charges
totaling P320,525.00 should not be charged personally to them, otherwise, a
Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be issued accordingly;

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to zero-out and
correct the balance of Loans Payable account presented in the FS and
henceforth, strictly observe the presentation requirements under IPSAS 2 in
the preparation of the Statement of Cash Flows; and

c. MPDC to implement strict procurement planning and validation procedures to
ensure that future development projects are free from legal impediments
before securing financing, thereby avoiding unnecessary financial
obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
the MENRO to come up with a report on the status, condition, location,
accountability and/or other relevant information needed to ascertain the existence
and conditions of the biological assets, otherwise prepare a journal entry to
derecognize the account balance from the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Pass a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the unliquidated PCF to Other
Receivables account; and

b. Coordinate with the former AO to immediately submit liquidation documents,
if the funds have already been expended, otherwise, refund the amount to the
Municipal Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to ensure the
optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent DF in the succeeding period by
including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready in compliance with the provisions set forth in DBM-DOF-
DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Local Finance Committee to propose fiscal policies and measures aimed at
improving the financial performance of the Municipality; and

b. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the
expenditures, ensuring they do not exceed the total income realized during the
year to avoid incurrence of net loss.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the BAC to immediately submit to the COA Audit Team all lacking documents
necessary to establish the regularity, validity and propriety of the disbursements,
otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in audit. Henceforth, the Municipal
Accountant should ensure that all payments to suppliers and contractors are
properly supported with complete documentation before processing of claims to
avoid audit suspensions and/or disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate return of the unexpended fund
transfers totaling P3,082,976.56 to the respective SAs or National Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Coordinate with the concerned AOs to immediately liquidate the outstanding
CAs, to prevent the withholding or suspension of their salaries for non-
compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and

b. Refrain from processing the grant of additional CAs to officers and employees
unless their previous CAs have been accounted and liquidated.

b. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the accounts, and
henceforth, strictly observe proper recording procedures to ensure the accurate
presentation of transactions in the books of account.

c. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the granting of CAs to
officers and employees who are either not duly designated as DOs, not
permanent employees of the LGU, and no approved fidelity bond; and

b. Designate additional Special Disbursing Officers (SDOs), when necessary,
for the grant of additional CAs intended for special projects/activities,
ensuring that they are properly bonded and permanent employees.

d. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to enter into a Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with a gasoline station to enable the procurement of POL
products through negotiated procurement - direct retail purchase, pursuant
to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and

b. Municipal Accountant to enforce that all procurement of goods/gasoline be
paid by checks directly to suppliers except for petty disbursements out of
petty cash fund in pursuance of COA Circular No. 97-002 so that appropriate
taxes could be withheld from suppliers and could be remitted to the BIR.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to immediately convene to plan or strategize the preparation of the books and records to conduct the preliminary activities in the one-time cleansing of PPEs. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 39 to 41, Part II of this Report

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the unrecognized depreciation on depreciable PPEs totaling P828,950,213.00 by debiting the Government Equity account and crediting the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation accounts.
observations.managementAction: The General Service Office (GSO) and Accounting Office immediately convene to have started plan or strategize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of all PPEs owned by the City in order to resolve the recurring issues on the discrepancy between the property records and accounting records and to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. For preparation of the RPCPPE. The City Accountant has prepared the JEV Nos. 100-2024-06-31746, 100-2024-06-31749, both dated June 20, 2024, to record the unrecognized depreciation of PPEs.
observations.reason: Composition of Inventory Committee has been revised. Further, the RPCPPE was not submitted before the set deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. Gather and present to the Audit Team the necessary documents that will substantiate the claims totaling P49,210,396.60, otherwise make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and

b. Strictly ensure that subsequent recording of Accounts Payable will be based on the relevant supporting documents attached to the claims instead of the Obligation Request submitted to the City Budget Office.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant immediately submit the necessary documents to the Audit Team the prepared JEV Nos. 100-2024-12-34227, 100-2024-12-34357 and 100-2024-12-34355 dated December 31, 2024 to record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements. The City Accountant has ensured that the recording of Accounts Payable will be based on valid claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. Prepare the necessary journal entry voucher to record in the books the noted book reconciling items totaling P733,526.38; 

b. Revert to the Cash in Bank - LCCA and record to the corresponding liability accounts the stale checks totaling P876,617.01; and

c. Henceforth, ensure that valid reconciling items and stale checks included in the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) are immediately adjusted in the books in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has prepared the necessary JEV Nos. 100-2024-03-31033 dated March 27, 2024, 300-224-03-01250 and 300-2024-03-01257 both dated March 7, 2024 to record noted book reconciling items. The City Accountant has caused the reversion pertaining to the stale checks. The City Accountant has ensured that valid reconciling items and stale checks included in the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) are immediately adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and City Budget Officer to submit a written explanation to the COA Audit Team regarding the non-submission of APP, for the latter’s evaluation; 

b. City Budget Officer to strictly enforce to the end-user or implementing units the preparation of PPMPs through different memorandum, Program, Activity, and Projects (PAPs) in order to facilitate the preparation of the City’s APP; and 

c. BAC to ensure that: 

   ● All procurements that will be undertaken are in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes thereto, pursuant to Section 7.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and 

   ● Similar items requested for the same PAP will be consolidated to avoid the splitting of procurements, which is prohibited under Section 54.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise the subject transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and City Budget Officer have submitted the written explanation regarding the non-submission of APP. The City Budget Officer has strictly enforced the submission of PPMPs through different memorandum. The BAC has ensured that all procurements were in accordance with the approved APP and has ensured avoidance of splitting of procurements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to:

a. Provide written justification to the Audit Team on why the subject contracts were awarded to unregistered suppliers who were not PhilGEPS-registered, otherwise the subject transactions will be disallowed in audit; 

b. Ensure that subsequent procurements of goods through Shopping and Small Value Procurement will be awarded to suppliers registered with the PhilGEPS pursuant to Section 54.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and 

c. Scrutinize and validate the technical, legal and financial eligibility documents submitted by the winning bidders in order to ensure that a contract will be awarded to a technically, legally and financially capable supplier, contractor or consultant.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that awarded contracts were given to PhilGEPS registered suppliers. The BAC also scrutinized and validated the technical, legal and financial eligibility documents of the winning bidders in order to ensure capability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC and City Engineer to coordinate with each other with regard the formulation of an improved procurement plan which will ensure that all priority development projects of the 20 percent DF will be implemented during the year in compliance with Section 3.2.3 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 and in order that the City's constituents will immediately benefit from the timely implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and City Engineer have coordinated with each other and ensured formulation of an improved procurement plan and implementation schedule for the priority development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF the subject disbursements and to strictly utilize the fund for programs and projects which are supportive of disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Item 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO has guaranteed to refrain from charging disbursements which are not supportive of disaster risk management activities to the LDRRMF and to strictly utilize the funds for the subject purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. Ensure that only one disbursing officer is designated for each specific disbursing purpose, pursuant to Section 108(A)(6) of the LTOM 2nd Edition, 2019, Book III; 

b. Direct the City Treasurer to require all the designated disbursing officers to maintain cashbooks; and 

c. Require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to ensure that the cash advances granted to the disbursing officers do not exceed the maximum cash accountabilities covered by fidelity bond and that all the designated disbursing officers are properly bonded as such.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor has issued designations for all disbursing officers and special disbursing officers. The City Treasurer has required each designated disbursing officer to maintain cashbooks. The City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer has ensured that disbursing officers are properly bonded and that they do not exceed the maximum cash accountabilities covered by their respective fidelity bonds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. Ensure that all City cash advances, except for petty cash fund, are fully liquidated at year-end and any unexpended balance shall be refunded by the accountable officer; 

b. Require every City official who will receive cash advance to strictly observe the prescribed period of liquidation of his/her cash advance to safeguard the cash from the risk of misuse or loss; and 

c. Record in the books the liquidation of cash advances only upon verification of the completeness of the supporting documents, which includes the official receipt issued for the excess cash advance returned by the accountable officer, if any.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant ensured that all cash advances, excluding petty cash, were fully liquidated at year-end and any unexpended balances were refunded by the accountable officers. The City Mayor has reminded all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. The City Accountant has ensured completeness of liquidation papers before recording them in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. Require the concerned City officials to submit immediately to the COA Audit Team the necessary documents that will support the validity of the pertinent reported expenses totaling P666,118.86, otherwise the subject payments will be suspended in audit; 

b. Ensure that all subsequent grant and other city cash advance for financial assistance will be supported with duly approved payroll or list of payees indicating the amount to be paid; and 

c. Henceforth ensure that every City official who will receive cash advance is well oriented in the documentary requirements needed in the liquidation of his/her cash advance, as prescribed in the pertinent sections of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2023-004, and ensure their completeness and validity prior to liquidation to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has required the concerned city officials to submit immediately to the COA Audit Team the necessary documents that will support the validity of the reported expenses. The City Accountant has created a standard payroll format for financial assistance and has oriented city officials of the documentary requirements needed in the liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Engineer (a) formulate and implement an action plan to fast-track the completion of the six projects amounting to P95,924,748.39 and thereafter, ensure strict compliance with the guidelines on the issuance of suspension orders provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (b) City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that necessary detailed engineering activities are conducted prior to procurement of infrastructure projects; and (c) BAC to require the submission of certification from the CEO that detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted by the latter.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the details in the CIP Subsidiary Ledgers and reclassified the aforementioned completed infrastructure projects to their appropriate PPE accounts and provided the corresponding depreciation expense. Reclassified the aforementioned infrastructure projects and provided the same with depreciation expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant draw JEVs to adjust the aforementioned accounts and ensure that appropriate accounts are used in recording warranty security and retention money, in accordance with the RCA for LGUs, as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: JEVs were drawn to adjust the deficiency by debiting the account Other Payables and crediting the account Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable by P273,709,549.17 and ensure Warranty security and retention money are properly recorded under the appropriate account in the subsidiary ledger and financial statements.
