observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the AOs to immediately liquidate their outstanding CAs and henceforth ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; that the City Accountant refrain from granting additional CAs to any official or employee without liquidating prior cash advances and instead designate additional SDO for the grant of additional cash advances for special activities/projects; and that the City Mayor direct all AOs and employees to ensure liquidation of CAs within the prescribed period and the City Accountant to send regular demand letters to AOs to ensure timely compliance and remind that salaries may be withheld for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The AOs were directed to immediately liquidate outstanding CAs and henceforth ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002. AOs with outstanding cash advances were not granted additional funds until their previous advances were fully liquidated and properly accounted for. The AOs and employees were directed to ensure liquidation of CAs within the prescribed period and the City Accountant to propose achievable measures to resolve delays, including sending demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to terminate the time deposits under SEF and use the funds for the projects enumerated in Section 100 (c) of RA No. 7160 and disburse them for the different schools of the City as may be determined by the Local School Board (LSB).
observations.managementAction: Facilitated the termination and transfer of Time Deposit of Special Education Fund to Current Account. The City Treasurer facilitated the termination and transfer of the time deposit under SEF and used the funds for the projects as determined by the Local School Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to: (a) formulate and implement an action plan to fast-track the completion of the six projects amounting to P95,924,748.39 and thereafter ensure strict compliance with the guidelines on the issuance of suspension order under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (b) that detailed engineering activities be conducted prior to procurement; and (c) that the BAC require certification from the CEO that detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted.
observations.managementAction: The CEO worked out an action plan to ensure that the remaining suspended projects would resume and be completed according to its revised target completion dates. Detailed engineering assessments were carried out to identify issues and corresponding actions to be taken. The CEO has taken steps to ensure that site validation is conducted before procurement of projects and certification that detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the DRRM Officer and City Accountant to ensure the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF is submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; that the LDRRM Council decide on the use of the unexpended balance and review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to incorporate PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE; and that the City Accountant maintain the SLs of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF by year of transfer to facilitate monitoring of the five-year period in which these funds shall remain in the Special Trust Fund and reversion thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF are being submitted before the 15th day after the end of each month. The PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF are being identified and incorporated into the CY 2025 LDRRMF Investment Plan. The personnel in charge of the SLs maintained the SLs of the unexpended LDRRMF by year of transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City GAD Focal point System and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to undergo trainings to build their competence in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for the purpose of GAD budget attribution and to apply the HGDG tool.
observations.managementAction: Acknowledged the need for continuous training on Gender and Development, particularly in using the HGDG tool. The GFPS and TWG attended trainings and orientation sessions to build their competence in the conduct of gender analysis and the proper application of the HGDG tool. They have undergone trainings on Gender and Development.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately remit to the BIR the unremitted taxes totaling P2,414,547.36 to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges due to late remittances of taxes; and henceforth, remit all taxes withheld by the City within the prescribed period of 10 days following the end of the month as required under Section 5(A) paragraph 3 of BIR Regulation No. 11-2018. Otherwise, all penalties and surcharges that may be incurred due to late remittances of taxes will be the personal liability of the City Treasurer in accordance with Section 251 of National Internal Revenue Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Remitted the balance of P2,414,547.36 to the BIR and ensured monthly withheld taxes were remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer (CGSO) and City Accounting Office to reassess and adjust the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) since the original timeline of the activities did not proceed as planned, then proceed with the enforcement of One-Time Cleansing of PPE using the procedural guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006, specifically to address the issues on the unreconciled balances of PPE in their records and the unaccounted PPE or those which were only presented in the lump-sum amounts, with the following activities:
observations.managementAction:
i. Section 6.3 - The Inventory Committee completed the inventory count per RPCPPE with property Reconciliation existence, condition between records was made in CYs 2023 and 2024.
ii. Section 7.0 - Disposition for non-existing/missing PPEs together with other concerned identified all missing/ non-existing PPEs without available records of accountability during the inventory count.
iii. Section 8.0 - The City derecognized non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and PPEs, recorded from previous years, without available records of accountability, through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2023-12-83747 dated December 31, 2023, to arrive at a PPE balance that is verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
iv. Section 9.0 - Accounting entries and disclosure in the notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare the BRS and submit copies, along with all the necessary supporting documents, to the Audit Team for verification within ten days after the end of each month. Likewise, draw JEVs to take up all valid reconciling items requiring adjustments and corrections in the books, to ensure the reliability and accuracy of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office submitted copies, their monthly BRS to the COA Audit Team. Likewise, draw JEVs to take up all valid reconciling items requiring adjustments and corrections in the books, to ensure the reliability and accuracy of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 43 to 44, Part II of this Report For preparation of Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: There were bank credit memoranda amounting to P1,470,000.00 that were not recorded in the books at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method, whereby purchases of inventories shall first be recorded under the proper Inventory account, and to recognize the expenses only upon issuance thereof to end-users and submission of the necessary supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO after a careful assessment of effectiveness, risk exposure, and practical considerations, properties were insured with the GSIS. Priority was given to assets that are high-value or at higher risk of damage or located in hazard-prone or vulnerable areas. (Note: this line seems misplaced in the table but management reported actions to implement inventory/perpetual method.)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a. CGSO to prepare and maintain the SPC for each class of Semi-Expendable Property, the RegSPI for the issuance, return, reissue and disposal of these items and the RSPI to summarize all issued properties, and submit the same to the City Accounting Office to be used as the basis for preparing the JEV to recognize the property issued; and

b. City Accountant to prepare the SPLC, which includes the acquisition, description, custody, impairment, issue/ transfer/ disposal, repair history and other information about the property to establish the validity and reliability of the recorded amounts in the books.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO maintained SPC for Semi-Expendable Properties, the RegSPI and RSPI for the year. The City Accounting Office prepared the SPLC for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all taxes withheld by the City as withholding agent of the BIR are properly and promptly remitted in full in accordance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to make representation with respective source agencies and/or the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) to determine the programs, activities and projects (PAPs) that could be funded out of the P715,584.00 and to ensure the implementation of the pertinent PAPs during the year. Further, immediately remit the unexpended balance of the transferred funds totaling P44,305.18 to their respective source agencies or the BTr.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized amounts were returned to the respective source agencies during the year. Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City General Services Officer to properly maintain and regularly update the SCs to monitor and control all procurements and issuances of supplies and materials, to prepare the monthly SSMI at the end of every month summarizing the approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), and to submit these records and reports to the City Accountant as the basis for recording the procurement and issuances in the books;

b. City Accountant to record all deliveries of supplies and materials in the appropriate SLCs upon the receipt of Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) and other supporting documents, then prepare the JEVs to take up in the books the: (1) procurement and (2) issuances based on the submitted SSMI at the end of each month; and

c. Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of all inventory items on a semi-annual basis and submit the corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction: a. The CGSO prepared and maintained SLCs and submitted SSMI to the Accounting Office during the year. b. The City Accountant recorded deliveries, prepared appropriate SLCs and JEVs upon receipt. c. Physical count was not necessary during the year as there were no inventory balances at the end of June and December 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CGSO to prepare the Property Insurance Fund (PIF) listing all the insurable properties of the City and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and GIF of the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year, and insure them with the GSIS in compliance with the foregoing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, after a careful assessment of effectiveness, risk exposure, and practical considerations, properties were insured with the GSIS. Priority was given to assets that are high-value or at higher risk of damage or located in hazard-prone or vulnerable areas.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Human Resource and Management Officer (CHRMO), in coordination with the Department Heads of the City, to:

a. Ensure that the contracts of the JO clearly provide their job descriptions, duties and expected deliverables against which their performance and accomplishments can be evaluated; and

b. Study the City's manpower needs and hire JO workers based on specific requests from concerned Department Heads who shall certify that his or her office needs to hire a JO worker for an identified piece of work.
observations.managementAction:
a. Hiring of JO workers were supported with approved contracts which clearly provides job descriptions, rates, and deliverables, among others.
b. Hiring of JO workers were supported with requests from the concerned departments to justify the hiring of JO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to increase the amount of bond of the AOs, who have been receiving a large amount of cash advances, pursuant to Section 101 of PD No. 1445 and BTr Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019, in order to protect the interest of the City Government, otherwise, limit the amount of their cash advances up to the extent of the maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. The fidelity bonds of the four AOs were increased according to the amount of cash advances granted to them as disbursing officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Development Council, the City Planning and Development Coordinator and the OIC-City Engineer to:

a. Immediately address the issues and concerns on the four projects and proceed with their implementation so that the benefits therefrom could be enjoyed by the City’s constituents in a timely manner; and

b. Henceforth, thoroughly review all development projects to ensure that all issues and concerns relating to the projects are addressed and resolved prior to their inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, ensuring proper facilitation of project completion on time.
observations.managementAction: The four infrastructure projects were implemented and completed during the year. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 9, Page Nos. 58 to 60, Part II of this Report For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Chairperson of the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to conduct gender analysis through the administration of HGDG tool from the planning and budgeting phase to the implementation, monitoring and evaluation phase, apply the results thereof in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the accompanying GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) and the identification of strategies to address gaps or missing elements, if any, to properly determine and gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of the GAD programs and activities and ensure that gender issues were addressed accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person conducted gender analysis using HGDG on applicable PAPs during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain the lapsing schedule for all the PPE of the City, complete with all necessary information to arrive at an accurate and correct balances of PPE accounts, and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of the PPE, expense, and Retained Earnings accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had prepared and submitted the lapsing schedule under Special Education Fund (SEF) last March 18, 2024 to the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. City GSO and City Accountant reconcile their records and reclassify as SEP the PPE with acquisition cost of less than P50,000.00 per unit in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004; and b. City Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to adjust the appropriate inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City GSO forwarded the List of SEPs to the City Accounting Office on January 3, 2024 for their reference in the reconciliation. The City Accounting Office prepared Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) on April 30, 2024 (JEV Nos. 04-2809 to 2810), May 31, 2024 (JEV Nos. 05-3935 to 3936) and December 31, 2024 (JEV Nos. 12-10347 to 10358 and 12-10363 to 10367) to record the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Certified List of RPT Taxes Due and Collectible for the year, based on the City Assessor’s Assessment Roll; and the City Accountant to set-up the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of every year based on the Certified List of Real Property Taxes Due and Collectible and reconcile any discrepancy with the Certified List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies to ensure the accuracy of the said receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer submitted the necessary documents and the City Accounting Office set-up the RPT and SET receivables by JEV No. 01-002 dated January 2, 2024 after adjustments and reconciliation of accounts were done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to prepare and furnish the City Accountant the Schedule of Unreleased Checks as of year-end for the restoration/reversion of Cash in Bank-LCCA and recognition of payable/liability.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer prepared and furnished the Schedule of Unreleased Checks for the year-end to the City Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to strictly deposit intact all daily collections with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) within the day or not later than the next banking day as required under PD No. 1445 and MNGAS for LGUs. Also, advise to establish strong internal controls in handling of cash.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer issued a Memorandum Order dated January 23, 2024 requiring all revenue collectors to remit their daily collections on the same day of collection. The City Treasurer also updated COA on the progress and provided reports/credit memos of matured time deposits. Examination of the ONI liquidating officer was conducted which showed collections were deposited in next banking day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to terminate TDs under SEF and make the same readily available to support programs and projects under the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Terminated time deposits under SEF. The City Treasurer updated COA on progress of TD termination by providing reports and credit memos of redemption/matured time deposits from DBP - Dasmariñas branch.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Engineer to coordinate with each other to facilitate the full settlement and proper liquidation of the fund, in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and MOA dated April 26, 2021.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Engineer settled the amount to the Contractor and submitted the liquidation report to TIEZA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to require the contractor to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit or bank guarantee or surety bond of equivalent value from a commercial bank or surety company; otherwise the advance payments shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Engineer submitted the copy of the advance payment bond to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct that all CAs and liquidation reports be supported with proper documentation, stop granting CAs for travel under one representative, and observe practice of granting cash advance per individual.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted a letter on March 15, 2024 relative to the liquidation of CAs assuring compliance. Verification by Audit Team showed City was already complying with recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior year recommendation that the City Mayor direct the M&E Team and other concerned officials to comply with establishment of a GAD M&E System as required under JMC and to develop system framework, draft guidelines, seek approval, and initiate implementation.
observations.managementAction: Meetings were held with concerned departments to identify gender issues. Capacity building seminar/training on GAD M&E was conducted and GFPS formulation activities performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain the lapsing schedule for all PPE items and prepare necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office prepared and submitted the lapsing schedule under SEF last March 18, 2024 to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City GSO and City Accountant reconcile records and reclassify SEPs, and City Accountant prepare adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: List of SEPs forwarded to City Accounting Office on January 3, 2024. City Accounting Office prepared JEVs April 30, May 31, and December 31, 2024 to adjust SEPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended City Treasurer prepare and furnish Certified List of RPT Taxes Due and Collectible and City Accountant set-up RPT and SET receivables and reconcile discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: City Treasurer provided Certified List and City Accounting Office set-up RPT and SET receivables by JEV No. 01-002 dated January 2, 2024 after adjustments and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to cause the immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the GF the AP that have long been outstanding for at least two years and which could not be supported with complete documentation. In addition, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all supporting documents necessary to prove the validity of the recorded AP, otherwise make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements. Further, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to ensure that the projects are already complete prior to issuing certification to that effect to prevent unsubstantiated or premature recording of AP by the City Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction:
Management Implemented
processed and paid 25.59 percent, amounting to P5,090,227.85, of the long outstanding AP. Additionally, checks which worth P1,108,450.32 that had been stale for at least two years were reversed to the unappropriated surplus in the GF, SEF, and TF. The remaining balance of P6,778,000.00 consists of liabilities incurred from the expropriation of several parcels of land.

In CY 2024, the City Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and supporting documents for the paid transaction totalling P109,353,676.98 were submitted to the Audit Team. The amount of P8,153,798.11 was reversed to the unappropriated surplus in the GF and SEF. Furthermore, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to ensure that the projects are already complete prior to issuing certification to that effect to prevent unsubstantiated or premature recording of AP by the City Accounting Office. The Statements of Work Accomplished were used as the basis for recording AP in year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer, City Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, for evaluation of the Audit Team, a written justification and all the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 89-296 and the Manual on Disposal of Government Property to justify and support the disposal/demolition of various infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures and the dropping from the books of the corresponding PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The written justification and all the necessary documents required under Section C of Part II of the Manual on Disposal of Government Property were submitted to the Audit Team.

The City should take note that the disposal committee has facilitated the disposal of the first batch of the remaining unserviceable PPE and 36 items amounting to P6,966,026.38 were disposed on March 31, 2024. The second batch of disposal consists of 10 items amounting to P780,300.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. City GSO and City Accountant to reconcile the balances of their respective records of inventories; and b. City Accountant to adjust in the books the balances of semi-expendable properties already issued to end-users/ beneficiaries using the reports submitted by the GSO.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO and Accounting Office have reconciled their books as of year-end.

The Accounting Office adjusted the issuance of semi-expendable properties already issued to end-users/beneficiaries using the reports submitted by the GSO. The adjustment which amounted to P3,913,025.00 was covered by Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2024-01-04 dated January 9, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to ensure the timely release of the barangays’ shares from RPT collections in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer's Office instructed the Land Tax Division to expedite the preparation of report of RPT collection at the end of each quarter to meet the prescribed period of five days after the end of each quarter for the release of the barangay shares.

The City Treasurer's Office and the City Accounting Office agreed that reconciliation will follow, and adjustment will take effect on the next payment to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to require every Accountable Officer (AO) who will receive a cash advance to strictly observe the prescribed due date for the liquidation of his/her cash advance, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and LTOM 2nd Edition, 2019, Book III, to safeguard cash from the risk of misuse or loss.
observations.managementAction: The policy of requiring promissory note filled-out by the AOs indicating that the amount of cash advance will be deducted from their salary if the liquidation report was not submitted to the Accounting Office after the due date, was implemented in CY 2024. Consistent follow-ups were also made to the AOs with cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Require the City Health Officer to strictly require from the suppliers the CPR from the BFAD; and b. Require the inspection and acceptance committee to submit the AIR to COA within 24 hours from receipt of the deliveries as prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction:
All medicine purchased were submitted with CPR.

All pending AIR were submitted to COA within 24 hours from receipt of deliveries as prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enforce timely project implementation by requiring the City Engineer, in coordination with the City Planning and Development Officer, to: a. Conscientiously carry out detailed engineering to address any issues on the technical viability of the project including projects implementation-ready before they are posted for bidding; b. Thoroughly review and evaluate the Program of Works to ensure that project designs and estimates are properly prepared and suit the needs of end-users and that all phases of the projects are covered to minimize time extensions; and c. Strictly monitor and supervise the status/progress of projects being implemented.
observations.managementAction:
The City Engineering Office (CEO) created accurate and detailed plans to address any issues on the technical viability of the project including project drawings, engineering drawings, and architectural plans, meeting the provided specifications of the projects of the City.

The CEO also created accurate timelines and Program of Works, for each project with key milestones and deadlines, with time allowance for revisions.

Respective engineers are also assigned to specific projects to be able to monitor and supervise the timely implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to compute, withhold and remit to the BIR the taxes due on the honoraria of chairman, secretariat and members and members of BAC and TWG whose total annual taxable income exceed the P250,000.00 threshold, in accordance with pertinent BIR laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office has already computed the withholding taxes for the chairman, secretariat, and members of BAC and TWG for CY 2023.

The corresponding withholding taxes on the honoraria for January to April 2023 were withheld in July 2024 and remitted in August 2024. The withholding taxes for October-December 2023 were also withheld in July 2024 and remitted in August 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (CDRRMC), through the City’s Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO), to include under separate caption the unexpended balances of LDRRMF in previous years and the programs or activities which will be charged to the funds as determined by the CDRRMC and approved by the Sangguniang Panglunsod (SP) in the Annual LDRRMFIP to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMC requested for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the previous years to be included under separate caption of the unexpended balances of DRRMF in previous years and the programs or activities to be charged to the funds as determined by the CDRRMC and approved by the SP in the Annual LDRRMFIP. The LDRRMFIP with separate caption of unexpended balances of DRRMF was approved per CDRRMC Resolution No. 03-2024 dated June 20, 2024 and is supported by City Resolution No. 2024-762 dated July 3, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. Property/Supplies custodian to furnish the City GSO copies of accounting records and documents and reports on the receipts and issuances of donated medicines and supplies for their recording to reconcile the discrepancies noted above with the records of the Accounting Office; b. OIC, Trece SSMI and TMMP Officer to issue the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) or other related document for every issued/sold item to be summarized weekly in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), certified correct by the City GSO and submit the same to the CAO for preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record cost of goods/items sold or issued, to reflect the correct balance of the Merchandise Inventory account for that period.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO prepared SSMI and provided copies to the CAO which was used by the latter as basis for recording of cost of goods sold with Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) 100-24-01-00270 dated January25, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the liabilities to their respective accounts, and observe the proper classification of accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU).
observations.managementAction: The CAO ensure to take appropriate action to revert the outstanding payables to the unappropriated surplus of the GF three months after formally informing the claimants of their unclaimed money.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant: a. In coordination with other Offices, inform in writing the concerned outstanding personnel and/or contractors about their unclaimed money with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the City in case proper claim be not made within reasonable period; b. To thereafter cause the immediate reversion of the dormant Other Payables to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund aged more than two years; and c. To prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the liabilities to their respective accounts, and observe the proper classification of accounts in accordance with the RCALGU.
observations.managementAction:
The CAO ensure to take appropriate action to revert the outstanding payables to the unappropriated surplus of the GF three months after formally informing the claimants of their unclaimed money.
The City Accountant had drawn the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the liabilities to their respective accounts with JEV No. 100-24-01-00255 dated January 24, 2024.
observations.reason:
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 6, Page Nos. 73 to 75, Part II of thisReport
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to: a. Conduct an in-depth review/analysis of the accounts with negative balances and make the necessary JEVs and adjusting entries in the accounting system to reflect the correct subsidiary ledger balances for all accounts; and b. Ensure that all entries made in the accounting system and records are accurate and produce reliable financial statements; in particular the financial statements for special accounts in the General Fund, with minimal human intervention.
observations.managementAction:
All 35 real accounts with negative balances were adjusted to reflect correct subsidiary ledger balances with JEV No. 100-24-01-00232 dated January 19,2024.
The Management ensured the accuracy and correctness of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Engineer, City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC), and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that detailed engineering was being carried out prior to awarding of contracts for infrastructure projects to ensure that issues on right of way, soil, relocation plan, and plan approvals are properly addressed prior to awarding and that suspension Orders and time extensions as provided under Sections 10.1 and 11.2 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are observed so that liquidated damages are imposed to contractors/suppliers incurring delays; b. City Engineer or any concerned officials to strictly observe the grounds on the issuance of suspension orders or time extensions as provided under R.A. No. 9184; and c. City Engineer or any concerned officials to submit draft suspension orders or time extensions to the City Mayor for her signature and approval prior to its release to the suppliers/contractors to ensure its validity.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer, Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC), and BAC ensured that detailed engineering was being carried out prior to awarding of contracts for infrastructure projects to ensure that issues on right of way, soil, relocation plan, and plan approvals are properly addressed prior to awarding of contracts.
observations.reason:
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 10, Page Nos. 85 to 87, Part II of thisReport
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Engineer and the City Accountant to submit written justification to the COA Audit Team on why the Extra Work Order exceeding 10 percent of the contract was not bid out and submit to the COA Audit Team the noted lacking documents to support the variation orders on eight projects, otherwise pertinent disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit; b. BAC and the City Engineer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Annex E, Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 relative to the issuance of variation orders and make sure that any cumulated positive change/extra work orders beyond 10 percent of the original contract price shall be bid out in another contract; and c. City Engineer to make sure that all documentary requirements enumerated in the Item 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for changes in the contracts were prepared before proceeding the works and attached to the disbursement vouchers submitted to the COA Auditor to support the changes made.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted a written justification letter dated March 7, 2024.
The Management ensured that variation orders were strictly in accordance with the provided rules and regulations.
The Management ensured compliance with the documentary requirements listed in the provided circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. City GSO to prepare the RSPI and issued semi-expendable properties totaling P2,150,573.00 and provide copies to the City Accounting Office for proper recording with JEV No. 100-24-03-00401 dated March 8, 2024 to record the same as expense using the Prior Period Adjustment account to reflect the correct balance of PED account; and b. City GSO to prepare and maintain SPCs and SPLCs for all semi-expendable properties in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022–004.
observations.managementAction: The City GSO prepared the RSPI and issued semi-expendable properties totaling P2,150,573.00 and provided copies to the City Accounting Office to prepare a JEV to record the same as expense using the Prior Period Adjustment account to reflect the correct balance of PED account.
observations.reason: No action has been taken regarding the preparation and maintenance of SPCs and SPLCs for all semi-expendable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Offices involved in the payment/disbursement process to monitor, possibly through the use of a routing slip, the flow of documents, to identify the cause of delays and/or areas that require improvement so that the City may comply with the aforementioned Manual and conduct its operations efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Usage of routing slips or other effective means to monitor payment/disbursement process to monitor the flow of documents was being implemented by the Management for proper monitoring of payment process flow.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Treasurer to obtain explanation from the Accountable Officer (AO) with overages in collection, and if the overages cannot be satisfactorily explained by the AO, forfeit the amount in favor of the City by issuing an Official Receipt and record the overage as “Miscellaneous Income”; b. City Treasurer to demand from the AO the immediate settlement of their unremitted collections, otherwise deduct the amount from their salaries, and thereafter make sure that overages and/or shortages are settled immediately once discovered; c. City Treasurer to institute control measures to minimize, if not totally eliminate, errors in the recording of collections, and thereby overages and shortages of Collecting Officers; d. City Accountant, in coordination with concerned offices, to send letters to creditors-employees at their last known address to inform them of their collectibles from the City and if no response is received on the deadline given, immediately revert these amounts to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with COA Circular No. 76-45; and e. City Accountant to prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the property tax share amounting to P101,814.50 to their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Management communicated with the concerned personnel thru a letter dated March 05,2024.
The City Treasurer issued a written demand letter stating the settlement of shortages incurred.
The Management assigned a focal person for the monitoring of the settlement of shortages and overages.
Letters to creditors-employees at their last known address to inform them of their collectibles from the City were sent accordingly.
The necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the property tax share were drawn with JEV No. 100-24-02-00375 dated February 21, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office to conduct preliminary inspection and submit punch-lists to contractors when they reach an accomplishment of 95 percent of the total contract amount to determine defects and thus, make necessary corrections, before work proper turnover and acceptance of the projects to the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Office conducts preliminary inspection at 95 percent completion and submit punch-lists to contractors on infrastructure projects when they reach an accomplishment of 95 percent to determine defects and thus, make necessary corrections, before work proper turnover and acceptance of the projects to the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; b. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain property cards based on the RPCPPE; c. Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards for each group of PPEs to facilitate the reconciliation and verification of the year-end balances; and d. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with reconciled balances of PPE. Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records between records cannot be made, consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; Not Implemented Annual physical count of PPE was not conducted for CY 2024. b. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain property cards based on the RPCPPE; Not Implemented Property cards were not maintained and updated by the MGSO. c. Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards for each group of PPEs to facilitate the reconciliation and verification of the year-end balances; Fully Implemented PPE Ledger Cards were maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office. d. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with reconciled balances of PPE. Not Implemented Due to non-preparation of RPCPPE for CY 2024, reconciliation between records cannot be made. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 37 to 40, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Annual physical count of PPE was not conducted for CY 2024. Property cards were not maintained and updated by the MGSO. Due to non-preparation of RPCPPE for CY 2024, reconciliation between records cannot be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense of the infrastructure asset, prepare the lapsing schedule, and draw necessary journal entry to take up the depreciation expense for the current year, establish the accumulated depreciation for the PPE account and for the depreciation expenses that should have been recorded in prior years, to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 17 and pertinent provisions of Item IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: All PPE were depreciated during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with the duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2024 to enable the latter Office to set up at the beginning of the year the RPT and SET Receivables for the current year correctly; and b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net difference of P13,866,974.79 between the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and the P27,765,662.37 discrepancy between the aggregate amount of these two accounts and the List of Unpaid Taxes as of December 31, 2023, and make the necessary adjustments in their records.
observations.managementAction: a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office the duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2024 to enable the latter Office to set up at the beginning of the year the RPT and SET Receivables for the current year correctly; and b. Municipal Accountant to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net difference. The certified list of delinquent taxpayers were submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accounting Office. The huge amount of variance between both records were reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their respective cash advances as soon as the purpose for which the cash advances were granted has been served by submitting the required Liquidation Reports and necessary supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office; settle their outstanding cash advances as of year-end and observe strict adherence to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the cash advances; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that additional grant of cash advances to officers and employees is processed only if their previous cash advances have already been accounted and settled, and that all must be liquidated by the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: a. Concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their respective cash advances as soon as the purpose for which the cash advances were granted has been served by submitting the required Liquidation Reports and necessary supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office; settle their outstanding cash advances as of year-end and observe strict adherence to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the cash advances; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that additional grant of cash advances to officers and employees is processed only if their previous cash advances have already been accounted and settled, and that all must be liquidated by the end of the year. There were no outstanding cash advances at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to expedite the implementation of the programs and projects for which the transferred funds are intended, or should it be determined that the projects are not anymore feasible or there had been no more use for the funds, to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the P350,058.19 to the respective source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended transfers were already returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain stock cards to monitor and control the procurement and issuance of supplies and materials, prepare the monthly SSMI at the end of every month summarizing all the expenses approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), and submit them to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording the expenditures/issuance made based on the submitted SSMI at the end of each month; and b. Municipal Accountant to record all deliveries of supplies and materials in the appropriate ledger cards upon the receipt of Inspection and Acceptance Report and other supporting documents and prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take up the procurement made in the books, and the expenditures/issuance made based on the submitted SSMI at the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: a. Municipal General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and submit SSMI to be used by the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for the recording the expenses. b. Municipal Accountant to record all deliveries of supplies and materials in the appropriate ledger cards upon the receipt of Inspection and Acceptance Report and other supporting documents and prepare the JEVs. Expenses were recorded on the basis of RIS attached to the disbursement voucher. No stock ledger cards were maintained for inventory accounts.
observations.reason: No stock ledger cards were maintained for inventory accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson to: a. thoroughly plan and review all development projects to ensure that all issues and concerns relating to the projects are addressed and resolved prior to their inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, ensuring proper facilitation of project completion on time; b. program the appropriated funds to development programs that are well-planned, procurement-ready, necessary, suitable and essential to promote the effective and efficient local governance and the general welfare of the constituents; and c. strictly adhere to the provisions provided by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020 to achieve the greater autonomy, transparency, and accountability in the City’s appropriation and utilization of its 20 percent Development Fund (DF).
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality implemented all projects under the 20 percent DF. The Municipality implemented all the PPAs under the 20 percent DF and ensured projects were planned and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the Municipality's 2023 LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years' LDRRMF and the continuing appropriations, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. Municipal Accountant to maintain SLs of the unexpended LDRRMF by year of transfer to facilitate the monitoring of the movements in the account and of the five-year period within which they shall remain in the Special Trust Fund, and consequently, the reversion of any unexpended balance to the GF after said period.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the LDRRMFIP included the balance from the Special Trust Fund and the Continuing Appropriation. The Municipal Accountant maintained SLs by year of transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC to ensure that all projects and programs in the LDRRMFIP are well-planned and properly implemented to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality implemented the majority of the PPAs for DRRM and these PPAs were implemented to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (MHRMO), in coordination with the Department Heads of the Municipality, to: a. ensure that the contracts of the JO clearly provide their job descriptions, duties and expected deliverables, their hiring and accomplishments, which their performance will be evaluated against, and the necessity of their hiring and regularity of payments to them would be evaluated; and b. study the Municipality's manpower needs and hire JO workers based on specific requests from concerned Department Heads or officials who shall certify that his or her office needs the services of a JO worker for an identified piece of work.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality revised contracts of JO personnel to include necessary information. Hiring of JO were requested by concerned Department Heads. Contracts of JO personnel were revised to include the necessary information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. BAC and other concerned officials to facilitate the review of the technical aspects of the contracts, detailing the necessary information in the determination of the reasonableness of the contract amount, the scope of work of the Contractors, and the supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to conduct a systematic and effective auditorial review and technical evaluation of the contracts; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of relevant supporting documents pertaining to garbage hauling and disposal.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality facilitated the review and submitted the contract and other supporting documents for hauling and disposal to the COA Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson of the TWG-GFPS to conduct gender analysis through the administration of HGDG tool from the planning and budgeting phase to the implementation, monitoring and evaluation phase, apply the results thereof in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget and the accompanying GAD Accomplishment Report and the identification of strategies to address gaps or missing elements, if any, to properly determine and gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of the GAD programs and activities and ensure that gender issues were addressed accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The programs included in the GAD Plan and Budget were based on gender analysis of the GAD Focal Person. Gender analysis through the HGDG were applied on the selection of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate and come up with an updated report on the status, condition, location, accountability and other relevant information needed for proper recording and/or derecognizing of biological assets in the books of accounts; b. Municipal Agriculturist to prepare a MOA duly signed by the Municipality and the current farmer-beneficiaries of the Municipal’s Cattle Dispersal Program; and thereafter ensure that perfected MOAs are prepared on every transfer or turn-over to establish obligation and accountability for the cattle; and c. Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents pertaining to the two death of two cows.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned officials coordinated and provided the updated report on the status, condition, location, and other accountability and/or information needed.
Relevant MOA for the remaining cattle were submitted for validation.
Submission of the documents pertaining to the two death of two cows was required in Item 4 of the MOA and thereafter ensure compliance of both parties with the requirements written in the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to any official, officer or employee with existing unliquidated cash advances. Additionally, consider designating additional Special Disbursing Officer/s (SDO) to handle cash advances for special activities/projects of the Municipality, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Additionally, designation of additional SDO to handle cash advances for special activities/projects of the Municipality shall be undertaken as the need arises

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to ensure the implementation of projects, programs, and activities allocated in the LDRRMFIP to enhance the disaster capabilities of the Municipality; b. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the prescribed format of the LDRRMFIP, the specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF, along with the continuing appropriations; c. MDRRMC to ensure that supplemental budgets or any revisions in the LDRRMF budget are supported by revised LDRRMFIP approved by the SB and the Municipal Mayor, which should then be the basis for the release and utilization of the LDRRMF for the identified new expenditure items; and d. MDRRMC to reprogram continuing appropriations to PPAs that are already included in the LDRRMFIP, and the revised LDRRMFIP be prepared and submitted, accompanied by copies of the corresponding approved MDRRMC and SB resolutions relative to the realignments and/or reprogramming of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to refrain from obligating and charging the LDRRMF for uniforms, which instead be charged against the annual clothing allowance and/or other budgetary account under the General Fund as may be allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations; otherwise, this transaction will be disallowed in audit; and
Municipal Budget Officer to revert the amount of P134,304.00 from the General Fund to the Trust Liability-LDRRM account through Obligation Request (ObR) No. MOOE 2024-04-1249 dated April 24, 2024.
observations.reason:
Full utilization of the implementation of fund funded under LDRRMF was not achieved during the year.
Previous years’ Unexpended Balances under the STF were not included in the LDRRMFIP.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 50 to 54, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal LDRRM Officer to refrain from obligating and charging the LDRRMF for uniforms, which instead be charged against the annual clothing allowance and/or other budgetary account under the General Fund as may be allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations; otherwise, this transaction will be disallowed in audit; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to revert the amount of P134,304.00 from the General Fund to the Trust Liability-LDRRM account through Obligation Request (ObR) No. MOOE 2024-04-1249 dated April 24, 2024.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal LDRRM Officer to refrain from obligating and charging the LDRRMF for uniforms, which instead be charged against the annual clothing allowance and/or other budgetary account under the General Fund as may be allowed under existing laws, rules and regulations; otherwise, this transaction will be disallowed in audit.
The Municipal Budget Officer reverted the amount of P134,304.00 to the Trust Liability-LDRRM account through Obligation Request (ObR) No. MOOE 2024-04-1249 dated April 24, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the bidding documents contain Instruction to Bidders, Bid Data Sheets, General Conditions of the Contract (GCC) and the Special Conditions of the Contract (SCC) to clearly and adequately define the obligations, duties and/or functions of the Bidder, in accordance with the Philippine Bidding Documents issued by the GPPB; b. BAC to ensure that the submitted performance securities were supported with an Official Receipt issued by the Insurance Company. They also mentioned that the erasures seen was treated as mere human error; c. BAC to evaluate the technical and financial documents of all bids submitted by the bidders using a checklist of the required documents using the non-discretionary “pass/fail” criterion; d. BAC Secretariat to post the Notice of Awards (NOA), Notice to Proceed (NTP) and the approved contracts in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed dates, in compliance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; e. BAC and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit written justification on why the submitted performance securities were accepted despite absence of material dates thereon and/or alteration of the contractor’s name, for evaluation of the Audit Team, and to take appropriate action against the subject contractor and other responsible officials, if warranted; and f. Municipal Accountant to make sure that all payments to suppliers and contractors are supported with complete and correct documents before processing them for payment; otherwise, transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Bidding documents were prepared in accordance with Philippine Bidding Documents issued by the GPPB.
BAC explained that the Performance Bond attached were supported by an Official Receipt issued by the Insurance company.
They also mentioned that the erasures seen was treated as mere human error.
BAC to evaluate the technical and financial documents of all bidders using the “pass/fail” criterion.
No actions taken by the Management to post the NOA, NTP and the approved contracts in the PhilGEPS website.
Established the correctness of various submitted documents.
observations.reason:
Payments were processed despite submission of incomplete supporting documents before processing them for payment. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 9, Page Nos. 56 to 62, Part II of this Report
For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated withmodifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant or his authorized representative to: a. submit all the lacking supporting documents enumerated in Item 4.1.2 above to the COA ATL; b. ensure that succeeding releases of advances for CF/IF are compliant with the requirements of Section 6.1 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JMC 2015-01, otherwise, this will not be stamped as “received” by the COA ATL or the authorized representative.
observations.managementAction:
Validated the submission of the lacking supporting documents on the grant of CF/IF in CY 2023 enumerated above to the COA ATL.
Submission of complete documentary requirements for grant of CF/IF were duly submitted to the Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. through the Acting Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), hire and/or promote qualified personnel to fill up vacant plantilla positions first before resorting to the hiring of JO workers so that deserving personnel may enjoy the benefits associated with plantilla positions; b. require the Acting HRMO and other concerned officials to come up with internal policy that will govern the recruitment of JO, including the terms and conditions of their hiring and other working arrangements, and the expected deliverables or accomplishments; and c. require the Acting HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to make sure that proper documentations, such accomplishment reports, authority to render additional work hours and claim the travelling allowances, among others, duly signed by the immediate supervisor and approved by the Municipal Mayor are attached to the claims.
observations.managementAction:
Filled up Plantilla positions to deserving and qualified job order workers.
The Acting HRMO and other concerned officials prepared proposed/revised contract and Memorandum No. 2023-02-002: Policy on the Hiring and Working Arrangements.
Compliance with submission of complete documentary requirements and attachments for claims.
observations.reason: The terms and conditions of the proposed/revised hiring of JO workers, their duties and responsibilities, and expected deliverables or accomplishments were stated in general terms. Although duly signed Extra Work Orders were prepared, they were not attached to Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for monthly wages of JO personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with his Co-Chairman and other members of the Local School Board:
a. Regularly monitor and evaluate the balance of the SEF to determine the actual unexpended balance thereof and ensure its inclusion in the SEF budget in the ensuing year; and b. Review and approve the SEF Budget for the ensuing year using the prescribed format of Form No. 1 on or before October 31 of the current year; and c. Ensure that the LSB Resolution and other pertinent documents are duly signed by the required numbers of LSB members.
observations.managementAction: Regular monitoring of SEF balances. Submitted LSB Resolutions with signatures of the LSB members.
observations.reason: Management did not regularly include the prior years’ unexpended balance of SEF. There is no indication that the SEF Budget was submitted on or before October 31 of the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer (GSO) to proceed with the preliminary and listing and comparison of PPE items between the latest Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and PPELCs but compared against the lump-sum PPE balances in the latter report from the inventory working papers, and henceforth follow the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and if applicable, take up the necessary accounting entries/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balances of amounts lumped under the “Unreconciled SL,” “Reconciling SL” for PPEs, and the like pursuant to Item 6.3.2(e) thereof or to immediately refer to the COA issues and concerns that the Municipality may encounter during the conduct of the one time cleansing of PPE, if any.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the appropriate adjusting entry pertaining to the payment for the acquisition of auxiliary customized play equipment costing P999,500.00 in CY 2023 which was subsequently donated to Barangay Poblacion I-A, Maragondon, and to provide the Audit Team with the copy of the corresponding Journal of Entry Voucher for review and validation.
observations.managementAction: Correction was already made thru the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2024 dated February 29, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to check all check disbursements based on the Report of Checks Issued prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and not on the approved disbursement vouchers with prepared checks alone, pursuant to Section 43 of the MNGAS, Volume 1, to ensure the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and liability accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the RCI together with the disbursement vouchers with issued checks on daily basis to facilitate the timely recording of the check disbursements by the latter.
observations.managementAction: RCI were now being prepared and submitted together with paid DVs daily to the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to take-up the necessary adjusting entry for the liquidation of the cash advances for Confidential Funds of the former Municipal Mayors totaling P6,220,000.00 in the books of accounts and to submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher/s supported with the copy of the duly received liquidation reports for review and validation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the three elected municipal officials to immediately submit to the latter the required liquidation reports on their cash advances totaling P130,000.00 and/or refund immediately to the Municipality’s cashier/collecting officer the unutilized cash advance, if there was any. Otherwise, immediately withhold the payment of their salary due to their failure to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Further we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly orient all Municipal officials and employees who will receive cash advance on the prescribed periods to be observed in the liquidation thereof, and the possible consequences in case of their failure to observe the same, as provided in Sections 5.1 and 9.3 of the COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The three Municipal officials had already liquidated their outstanding cash advances as of July 22, 2024. The Municipal Accountant also made JEV No. 100-2024 dated February 21, 2024 for the correcting entry.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Fully adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method by recording all the acquisitions of supplies and materials, except those purchased out of the Petty Cash Fund, to an Inventory account; b) Prepare and maintain SLCs for each type of supplies and materials for the proper recording of the receipts and issuances of the same and monitoring of their balances by the Accounting Office; c) Record the issuances of supplies and materials based on the SSMI to be prepared and submitted by the General Services Office; and d) Record the appropriate adjusting entry for the acquisition of PPE amounting to P126,684.00 which was improperly recorded to Other Supplies and Materials Expense and to provide the Audit Team with the corresponding Journal of Entry Voucher for review and validation. Henceforth, ensure that all transactions are recorded in the proper accounting to avoid similar misstatements in the succeeding periods.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to: a) Prepare and maintain SCs for each type of supplies and materials for the proper recording of the receipts and issuances of the same and monitoring of their balances by the General Services Office; and b) Prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to enable the latter to properly record the issuances of supplies and materials in the books of accounts.

Further we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of all the supplies and materials every semester and to submit the corresponding RPCIs to the Accounting Office and General Services Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of their respective records.
observations.managementAction: Correcting entry was already made thru JEV No. 100-2024 dated February 29, 2024. The Municipal Accountant also coordinated with the GSO, instructing them to properly prepare and submit the required SSMI, duly supported with RIS, in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the correctness of the recorded payables amounting to P650,000.00 for gasoline expenses by obtaining all the issued supplier’s delivery/sales invoices for fuel and gasoline which were delivered and billed on the account of the Municipality in CY 2023 but which remained unpaid as of year-end and prepare the appropriate adjusting entry for the said payables which are determined to be not valid claims.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the copy of all the AIR for goods and services which were delivered and accepted but which remained unpaid as of year-end to facilitate the recording of the same as payables of the Municipality in the books of accounts.

Further we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording as payables the claims which are based on estimates only and to record as payables the claims which are supported by issued supplier’s delivery/sales invoice and other documents which would establish its validity to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the Accounts Payable in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Correcting entry was already made thru JEV No. 100-2024 dated February 29, 2024. AIRs were properly and completely accomplished as to all the required information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor and other concerned municipal officials to: a) Take the necessary actions to expedite the registration of the titles on the five fully paid parcels of land totaling P20,456,000.00 under the name of the Municipality to safeguard its interest over these properties against possible third-party claims; b) Submit proof of payment of CGT and DST for the acquisition of land totaling P2,400,000.00; and c) Submit written explanation on why the total consideration per Deed of Sale and selling price per CAR for the partially paid land with check number 1033538 dated April 26, 2023, had a discrepancy amounting to P134,998.20, for review and validation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The concerned official was closely coordinating to a third party in-charge in the processing of payments for CGT and DST. The concerned Municipal Assessor had already retired and no documents related to the acquisition of land were handed over to the newly designated Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Municipal Engineer and the concerned Contractors to submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements for the subject payments totaling P6,947,288.93 for further evaluation, otherwise, the transactions will be suspended in audit.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the documentary requirements for payments of progress billing and final progress billing prescribed by the COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 are complete before certifying the DVs that the documentary requirements are complete, to avoid suspension of transaction in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer requested from the respective contractor the copies of the lacking as-built plans and pictures of the project while the concerned municipal employee is already validating DVs not supported with warranty securities and will locate and/or coordinate with the respective supplier pertaining thereto. The Municipal Accountant stated that she will strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the complete documentation before processing any claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to impose the defects liability period against the contractors by issuing the Certificate of Acceptance for completed infrastructure projects one year after the date of the issuance of the Certificate of Completion, in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 and the Local Government Units Procurement Manual.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was already being followed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractors to immediately install the project signboards of the four awarded and ongoing infrastructure projects totaling P7,960,635.82 within the vicinity of the project location considering that cost for the same was included in their contract cost.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the posting of the project signboard to ensure full transparency and accountability in the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The project signboards were already installed by the contractors. The recommendation was already being followed.

A separate plan for unexpended balances of LDRRMF from prior years was prepared. A copy of the Minutes of the Regular Meeting of the MDRRMC dated July 21, 2023 showing the approval of the Council on the proposed re-alignment of LDRRMF (CBMS) expenses was submitted to the Audit Team. Subsidiary ledgers were already maintained by year of transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator and all other concerned Municipal officials to have a consultation meeting with the RTF ELCAC and barangay officials of the Barangay Sta. Mercedes (Patungan) to properly evaluate the probability of implementation of the two projects chargeable against the LGSF - SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC within the validity period for the use of the funds or until December 31, 2024, and if determined not probable, the Municipality should immediately refund the amount received to the National Treasury, pursuant to Section 4.9 of the LBC No. 151 dated March 30, 2023.
observations.managementAction: P6,606,882.17 was refunded to the Bureau of Treasury paid under check no. 1117407 dated June 26, 2024.
