observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the Provincial Accountant to ensure that all taxes withheld by the Provincial Government as withholding agent of the BIR are properly and promptly remitted in accordance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012.
observations.managementAction: The Province withheld and remitted all taxes due to BIR during the year in compliance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012. The Province remitted the balance of P42,388,987.97 in January and February 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant to immediately remit all outstanding payables to the GSIS and henceforth, strictly comply with the IRR of RA No. 8291 by ensuring that the amount withheld from employees for their GSIS contributions and loan amortizations are remitted in full to the GSIS on or before the 10th calendar day of the month following the month to which the payments are due and applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Accountant stated that the amounts were being monitored and that some delays were due to the Covid-19 pandemic and restrictions to travel which affected the ability of offices to remit to GSIS. The Accounting Office assigned personnel to monitor past due accounts to GSIS and submitted a photocopy of correcting journal entry voucher for a recording error. They will write the GSIS to request waiver of penalties and will meet offices with late remittances.
observations.reason: The amounts remained outstanding in some offices as delays were due to travel restrictions imposed during the Covid-19 pandemic and shortage in manpower of the GSIS. Some remittances were validated as a recording error and corrected, but other arrearages remained and required coordination with concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant to cause the immediate remittance to the HDMF and PhilHealth or make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts as the case maybe.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Accountant, in coordination with the Provincial Treasurer, took action by revising few procedures to facilitate remittances to the subject agencies, giving more responsibility to the Provincial Treasurer in this regard pursuant to pertinent provision of RA No. 7160 which provides that the Treasurer shall take charge of the disbursements of all local government funds. As such, HDMF premiums and loans and PhilHealth contributions were remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Head, Provincial Project Management and Implementation Unit and other concerned Provincial officials to exercise due diligence and institute stricter control measures to prevent similar problems or issues with the contractors in future projects and ensure the timely execution of the Work and Financial Plan, most especially of ODA projects.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) asserted its responsibility to monitor not just ODA projects but all projects being implemented by the Province. Also, as another measure, the Provincial Project Monitoring Committee (PPMC) will also include all ODA-funded projects, if any, as top priority in its monitoring activities. The Province did not receive any ODA funds for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the Commission on Audit of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in the audit of the procurement of infrastructure projects and equipment to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the subject transactions.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted to the COA Audit Team and verified by the latter to be in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial SWMB (Department of Environment and Natural Resources - Bureau) thru the Provincial Government-Environment and Natural Resources Officer, to identify and propose viable alternative solutions to all issues that had been preventing the finalization of the Province’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan so that the plan could be immediately submitted to the NSWMC for review and approval.
observations.managementAction: The Province had already submitted its SWMP to the NSWMC (Department of Environment and Natural Resources- Bureau) on July 14, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to continue their efforts to fully establish the GAD M&E System of the Province as required under Sections 3.4, 5.1 and 5.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars No. 2013-01 and 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: The Province had already established a GAD M&E Team thru the issuance of Executive Order from the Provincial Governor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the COA Audit Team of the lacking documents and verified them to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the subject transactions.
observations.managementAction: During CY 2021, there were still some disbursements with no complete supporting documents. The Provincial Administrator and Provincial Accountant stated that the documents will be provided to the COA Audit Team once available.
observations.reason: There were still some disbursements with no complete supporting documents as of audit; management committed to provide the documents to the COA Audit Team once available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement under GPPB Circular 01-2020, for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted, although belatedly, documents and photos showing the posting of subject procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the General Service Officer to submit to the Auditor samples or photographs and the amended Acceptance and Inspection Reports indicating the complete specifications of the delivered PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The General Service Officer submitted to the Auditor samples or photographs and the amended Acceptance and Inspection Reports with complete specifications of the delivered PPEs.

Close coordination is being made by the two offices and are still exerting efforts to reconcile the amount.
The GSO had already complied with the recommendation as per submitted IIRUPs that were used as basis for dropping unserviceable PPEs totaling P27,555,458.18 as provided in the Manual on Disposal of Government Unserviceable Properties, and;
There were newly created positions in the City Accounting Office dedicated to the function as personnel assigned in the reconciliation and maintenance of PPE records were implemented.
The City Administrator as directed by the City Mayor is constantly monitoring the reconciliation of records of the City Accountant and GSO, and always reminding the two officials to speed-up in their reconciliation activity.
observations.reason: The reconciliation is still in process. Reiterated in audit observation no. 1, page 38 of Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to validate the above-mentioned discrepancies, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for proper recording to appropriate asset or expense account, and thereafter, submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers for the entries made. Henceforth, implement the perpetual inventory method of accounting for supplies/inventories, maintain supplies ledger cards and ensure regular reconciliation of records with the GSO.
We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services, the City Treasurer, the City Health and the City Welfare and Development Offices to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the following documents: RSMI supported with RIS for the issued supplies inventories on a monthly basis and the RPCI for reconciliation with the reported inventory balance in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO had submitted the actual Inventory of Report of Supplies and Materials on Stock as of December 31, 2021 on April 19, 2022 for Audit Team’s evaluation.
Likewise, the Accountable Officer (AO), in coordination with the City Accountant, had now been preparing RIS prior to the purchase of Accountable Forms, Plates, and Stickers.
Concerning the discrepancy in the report submitted by the AO to the City Accountant amounting to P4,865.00, the amount of P615.00 was included in the report but the actual usage and purchase of the Accountable Forms were being administered by the Office of the Civil Registrar. As for the overstatement of expense, the AO admitted that he erroneously encoded 100 booklets instead of 50 booklets. Correspondingly, the error was communicated and corrected by the City Accountant.
Moreover, the CSWDO mentioned that there were five wheelchairs donated and might have been the ones tagged in the RIS. Further, she clarified that their office had secured a certification from the GSO that the wheelchairs were already issued. The City Accountant clarified that donations must not be included in the RIS and the City shall revisit and analyze the RIS of the wheelchairs to confirm.
observations.reason: The GSO had already prepared and submitted the RSMI supported with RIS for the issued supplies inventory. The City Treasurer, City Health and Development Offices are yet to prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI supported with RIS for the issued inventories on a monthly basis and the RPCI for reconciliation with the reported inventory balance in the books.

observations.recommendation:
a) Payments were made for thirteen transactions aggregating P27,220,940.00 despite non-compliance with documentary requirements provided under Items 4.1 and 4.3 of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 01-2020, thus casting doubt on the validity of the supporting documents and propriety of the disbursements. We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to coordinate with responsible City officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in this audited transaction. Henceforth, ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the DVs before payment to facilitate the complete review of the validity and propriety of the disbursement-transactions.

b) Distributions/issuances of food packs to the frontliners were not properly monitored, resulting in a difference between the total food packs distributed and total recipients of 2,399 packs amounting to P183,450.00. We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned City officials/personnel who were in-charge of the receipts and distributions of food packs to the frontliners to submit written explanation, for the Audit Team’s further evaluation, on the unaccounted 2,399 food packs totalling P183,450.00. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance will be issued by the Audit Team.

c) The validity and necessity of the purchasing 3,300 pieces of backpack bags totaling P1,702,500.00 to put in the food and health packages for Day Care Children of the City was questionable in audit considering that the school bags were not among the COVID-19-related expenses enumerated under Item 3.5 of LBC No. 125, and their necessity and utility during the pandemic could not be categorically established, otherwise, the same will be suspended/disallowed in audit. We recommended that the City Mayor submit justification, for the Audit Team’s further evaluation, for procuring 3,300 pieces backpack bags considering that backpack bags were not among the COVID-19-related expenses enumerated under Item 3.5 of LBC No. 125, and that their necessity and utility during the pandemic could not be categorically established, otherwise, the same will be suspended/disallowed in audit.

d) Procurement of protective goods and testing kits aggregating P652,450.00 was made through Shopping or Small Value Procurement instead of the Emergency Procurement modality required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, thus procurement during COVID-19 pandemic was not expedited. We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC to undertake the procurement of COVID-19-related goods using the emergency procurement (Negotiated Procurement) modality required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 to ensure prompt deliveries of goods to beneficiaries.

e) The City was unable to fully comply with the posting requirements of the BGCM Monthly Utilization Report and the Status of the PPAs Implementation in its website and in at least three conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month, the proof of which should be submitted to the Audit Team. Likewise, it was recommended that all Notices of Awards, contracts or purchase orders related to emergency procurement totaling P34,903,738.13 be posted in the GPPB online portal and the City’s official website. We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Finance Committee to prepare the Monthly Utilization Report and the Status of BGCM Fund, and thereafter, update the posting of the same in the City’s official website and in at least three conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month, the proof of which was to be submitted to the Audit Team. Likewise, it was recommended that all Notices of Awards, contracts or purchase orders related to emergency procurement of COVID-19-related goods be posted in the GPPB online portal and the City’s official website to ensure transparency in the disbursements of the BGCM Fund.
observations.managementAction:
A formal written explanation on the actions taken by CGO-Cavite with regard to the findings on the deficiencies/errors on this audited transaction was submitted and received by COA on May 28, 2021 with all the necessary attachments as Annexes.

The CGO – Cavite ensured that all required supporting documents are attached to the DVs before payment to facilitate the complete review of the validity and propriety of the disbursement-transactions. Fully Implemented.

The CGO – Cavite ensured that Notice of Awards, contracts or purchase orders related to emergency procurement of COVID-19-related goods were posted in the GPPB online portal and the City’s official website. Fully Implemented.
observations.reason: Some actions were fully implemented (e.g., posting and compliance) and formal written explanations and attachments were submitted to COA on May 28, 2021. Other items (e.g., explanation on unaccounted food packs, justification for backpacks) were subject to audit evaluation and potential suspension/disallowance if not satisfactorily explained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to explain/justify the material discrepancies between the prices of the basic commodities procured by the City in response to the COVID-19 and their suggested retail prices issued by the authorized government agencies during the price freeze implementation, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. Henceforth, ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the DVs before payment to facilitate the complete review of the validity and propriety of the disbursement-transactions.
observations.managementAction: A formal written explanation on the actions taken by CGO-Cavite with regard to the findings on the deficiencies/errors on this audited transaction was submitted and received by COA on May 28, 2021, with all the necessary attachments as Annexes.
observations.reason: The formal written explanation and attachments were submitted to COA on May 28, 2021 for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
a. prepare and submit to the Office of the President the required Memorandum of Agreement, and the Completion Project Report, as proof of compliance therewith, and should be furnished the Audit Team; and
b. submit to the Audit Team the Mayor’s Permit and National Food Authority Registration of the supplier and other lacking supporting documents, and the written justification why the procurement was not included in the Supplemental Investment Program for COVID-19-related PPAs, for further evaluation of the Audit Team, to prevent the suspension of the transaction in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials prepared and submitted to the Office of the President the Memorandum of Agreement, copy of Mayor’s Permit and National Food Authority Registration of the supplier and other lacking supporting documents, and the written justification why the procurement was not included in the Supplemental Investment Program for COVID-19-related PPAs. These documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require all the concerned City officials and Punong Barangays to conduct coordinated investigation to enable them to establish the circumstances behind the 2,438 unaccounted packs of relief goods packages, and to submit to the Audit Team a written explanation/report thereon for further evaluation, otherwise, the amount of P833,796.00 will be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: A formal written explanation on the actions taken by CGO-Cavite with regard to the findings on the deficiencies/errors on this audited transaction was submitted and received by COA on May 28, 2021 with all the necessary attachments as Annexes.
observations.reason: The formal written explanation was submitted to COA on May 28, 2021; audit evaluation pending. Otherwise, the amount of P833,796.00 will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development Coordinator to indicate in the 20 percent DF the earliest timelines for the implementation of development programs, projects and activities (PPAs), and the BAC to align the timing of procurement processes to these timelines to ensure the optimum utilization of the Fund, pursuant to Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Section 7 of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and other concerned officials had already aligned the timing of procurement processes for the purpose as stated. The CPDO and the City Engineer were coordinating for the scheduling and implementation.

The City Budget Office allocated five percent of the Annual Budget for the LDRRMF. The CPDO and CDRRMO provided AIP. Through the guidance of the LDRRMC Chairperson, the City will endeavor to implement the identified PPAs and maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF-AIP to increase the capability and capacity of the LDRRM personnel. The Office will closely coordinate with the City Accounting Office for the immediate issuance of the copy of the LDRRMF utilization report for the inclusion of the unimplemented PPAs be included in LDRRMF-AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Office Orders or any equivalent document from the City Mayor, showing that the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay were authorized to report for work during the ECQ and Modified ECQ, otherwise, a Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance will be issued accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The officials submitted the Office Orders or equivalent documents from the City Mayor showing that the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay were authorized to report for work during the ECQ and Modified ECQ. These were provided to the Audit Team in April 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the City’s GFPS and the TWG are adequately trained to conduct gender analysis using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) assessment/scoring tool and that capability building activities be provided. Also ensure GAD allocation of at least five percent as required.
observations.managementAction: The City LCE issued Executive Order No. 1, s. 2021, reorganizing the GFPS Members and designating new set of GFPS-TWG. The City allocated P250,000.00 in the Annual Budget for Capability Building activity on GAD analysis and HGDG administration.
observations.reason: In-depth support and assistance were provided by the previous GAD-TWG Chairperson and DILG personnel during the completion of the City’s GPB for CY 2021. The City is yet to conduct the planned capability building activity to fully train all members.

observations.recommendation:

to make the necessary adjustments in the books.
We further reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the City
Mayor cause the close monitoring of the reconciliation between the records
of the City Accountant and the GSO until the PPE account balance is fully
reconciled.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to explain/justify the
material discrepancies between the prices of the basic commodities

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned City officials to:
a. prepare and submit to the Office of the President the required

and

Development Coordinator to indicate in the 20 percent DF the earliest

activities (PPAs), and the BAC to align the timing of procurement processes
to these timelines to ensure the optimum utilization of the Fund, pursuant to

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Budget Office to allocate no less than five percent of the Annual
Budget for the LDRRMF, in compliance with Section 21 of RA No.
10121;
b. City Planning and Development Office and the CDRRMO to provide

(AIP) for guidance and monitoring; and
c. HRMO to intensity its efforts to widely inform the public of the need of

compliance with Section 12 of RA No. 10121. It was also recommended
that qualified City personnel be assigned to the LDRRMO so that they
will gain the required two years working experience in that Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit
to the Audit Team the Office Orders or any equivalent document from the
City Mayor, showing that the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19
hazard pay were authorized to report for work during the ECQ and Modified
ECQ, otherwise, a Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance will be

GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to provide annual allocations for GAD PPAs

and the TWG are adequately trained to conduct gender analysis using HGDG
