We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert more effort to reconcile the difference of P20,039,399.26 existing between the accounting records and RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Effort had been made by the concern offices, however, difficulty of retrieving old records/documents are still ongoing.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation No. 1, page No. 41.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to exert more efforts to retrieve the records of the subject PPEs and provide detailed subsidiary records itemizing the composition of aforementioned PPE accounts to enable the computation of depreciation expense and to make the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: Effort had been made by the Accounting Office however, due to difficulty of retrieving old records/documents, depreciation for some properties are still ongoing.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation No. 2, page No. 42.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Development Council to scrutinize the projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund and ensure that they contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as prescribed by the aforementioned Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1. We further recommmended that the City Mayor and the City Development Council propose and facilitate the re-appropriation of savings from projects completed in prior years to other priority development projects that will bring about socio-economic benefits to the City and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2019, no action from the City Development Council to re-appropriate the savings from the implemented prior years’ projects to other priority projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation No. 3, page No. 44.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development Coordinator to indicate in the 20 per cent Development Plan the timelines for the implementation of development PPAs and the BAC to align the timing of procurement processes to these timelines to ensure the optimum utilization of the Fund pursuant to Section 5 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and Section 7 of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2019, projects were unable to increase the number of implemented/ finished projects during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation No. 3, page No. 44.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. direct the City Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert
more effort to reconcile the difference of P122,042,141.31 existing between
the accounting records and RPCPPE.

b. assign a capable official or employee to closely monitor and report on the
progress of the reconciliation being undertaken by the City Accountant and
the GSO until the PPE account balance is fully reconciled.

c. require the City Accountant to exert extra efforts to retrieve the records of
the subject PPEs and provide detailed subsidiary records itemizing the
composition of aforementioned PPE accounts to enable the computation of
depreciation expense and to make the necessary adjustments in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Coordinator to indicate in the 20 per cent Development Plan the timelines for the
implementation of development PPAs and the BAC to align the timing of
procurement processes to these timelines to ensure the optimum utilization of
the Fund pursuant to Section 5 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1 and Section 7 of RA No. 9184.

We further recommended that the City Mayor and the City Development Council
propose and facilitate the re-appropriation of savings from projects completed in
prior years to other priority development projects that will bring about socio-
economic benefits to the City and its constituents.

We recommended that the City School Board strictly adhere with the guidelines
provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017, particularly on
the formulation/ preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1 and the three-
year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of SEF
and preparation and submission of the quarterly and annual reports on SEF
utilization to the concerned offices.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to revert to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balance of the
CDRRMF in CY 2014 to make them available for other social services of the City,
subject to compliance with other necessary requirements, including the
enactment by the local sanggunian of an ordinance for that purpose.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:

a. the BAC and the City Engineer to ensure that site inspections and
investigations and other pre-procurement activities had been completely and
properly undertaken before starting the procurement process;

b. the City Engineer and other concerned officials to investigate further the
causes of delays in the completion of the subject projects and establish
whether there is unjustifiable fault on the part of the contractors and
therefore, should be imposed with liquidated damages accordingly;

c. the City Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the Suspension Orders for the
three projects, namely. Improvement of Mini Park at Muralla, Construction of
Concrete Ramp and Improvement of MRF and Construction of Senior High
School Laboratory (Phase I) Cavite National High School, otherwise the
transactions will be suspended in audit. Henceforth, it is recommended that
every revision to the contract such as contract time extensions, extra work
orders or variation orders, be also submitted to the Auditor for appropriate
review and evaluation; and

d. the City Engineer, the City Accountant and other concerned offices to ensure
that all suspensions of works of infrastructure projects strictly adhere with
Section 9.2 of Annex D of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to the
Audit Team the lacking supporting documents for donations/financial assistance
to facilitate the determination of the propriety and validity of the claims, otherwise,
the relevant expenses will be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, it is
recommended that the City Accountant ensure that all payments or releases of
government funds are supported with complete documents in compliance with
Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and other
concerned officials to adhere strictly to Section 93 of PD No. 1445 which requires
government agencies to make direct payments to suppliers and ensure that RA
9184, also known as the Government Procurement Reform Act, and BIR Revenue
Regulation 2-98 are not circumvented.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Executive Committee of the GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure that the GAD PPAs included in the GPB are
properly planned to facilitate effective and efficient implementation.

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the GFPS, through the GAD
Focal Person, to ensure that all PPAs included in the GPB have well-defined
gender issues or GAD mandate to be addressed.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments on the entry made for the undisposed PPE. Henceforth, draw Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after disposal, in accordance with the cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City General Services and CGSO had already made reconciliation of their records on unserviceable properties however, due to difficulty in retrieving old records, reconciliation could not be done immediately.
observations.reason: No actions were made by the City Accounting Office to make necessary adjustments in the books the disposed unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CSB strictly adhere to the guidelines provided under the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017, through the formulation/preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1 and a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of SEF and prepare and submit the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization to the concerned offices.
observations.managementAction: The CSB is already in the process of preparing project plans to be charged against the savings of prior years.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. No actions were undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the CDRRM Council to identify and incorporate under a separate caption in the CDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. We also recommended that the City Accountant be directed to revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balances of the CDRRMF in CYs 2012 and 2013 to make them available for other social services of the City after enactment.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRM Council had incorporated under a separate caption in the CDRRMFIP the projects and activities chargeable against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. Moreover, the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were already submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation. The City Accountant had reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balances of the CDRRMF in CY 2012 and 2013.
observations.reason: The City Accountant was unable to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balance of the CDRRMF of CY 2014, which was also beyond the allowable period of five years from its transfer to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC and its TWG to conduct a thorough post-qualification of bidders and evaluation of bids; disqualify contractors with simultaneous ongoing projects; and require the City Engineering Office to furnish copies of the Suspension Orders with supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, projects were unable to limit the awarding of projects to different bidders and had limited the projects. The BAC had limited the projects.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. No actions were immediately undertaken by the Office of the City Engineering hence Suspension Orders were not evaluated at the most appropriate time. Reiterated in audit observation No. 6, page No.48.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of all contracts and all supporting documents within five working days from their perfection/execution to the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001. We further recommended that the concerned City officials prepare all contracts in standard form as prescribed under RIRR of RA No. 9184 by indicating and specifying all relevant dates, the obligations of the contracting parties and the sanctions for non-implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: All contracts were submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from their perfection/execution. The City Accountant had prepared all contracts in standard form as prescribed under RIRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by indicating on the displayed signboards all relevant information relative to the implemented PPAs to fully inform the public and to effect full disclosure policy of the transactions governing public interest. Further, require such Office to submit at the beginning of the year a list of ongoing and to be implemented PPAs for the year and to inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the projects or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards are already posted on the project sites.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Office had installed signboards in all the project’s sites, by indicating therein the name of the project agency, name of the contractor and the project cost. The City Engineering Office submitted to the Audit Team copies of all contracts within five working days.
observations.reason: The City Engineering Office did not provide at the beginning of the year the list of to be implemented PPAs. The Office was unable to submit at the beginning of the year the list of to be implemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to require the submission of an irrevocable standby Letter of Credit, bank guarantee, or surety bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the City for advance payments to contractors and that claims for mobilization/advance payment are supported with all the required documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: All supporting documents pertaining to the mentioned transactions were already submitted and evaluated by the Audit Team. The City Accountant had required the contractor the submission of all supporting documents prior to the payment of advances to the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the City’s GAD Focal Person and the TWG are adequately trained to conduct gender analysis using HGDG and require them to attend GAD-related Trainings/Seminars in order that the PPAs attributed in the GAD Budget are gender-sensitive and responsive.
observations.managementAction: The City’s GAD Focal Person and the TWG had attended trainings and attended seminars using HGDG assessment/scoring tool.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City General Services Office to reconcile the Inventory Records and take up in the books the distribution of the welfare goods and reconcile the balances of Office Supplies Inventory and Accountable Forms Inventory with the Inventory Reports maintained by the City General Services Office.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office and the City General Services Office are still in the process of reconciling their records, however, due to manpower constraints and difficulty in retrieving records, the immediate reconciliation of the same cannot be made.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of records/documents, the reconciliation of both records was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting Office to provide the Accumulated Depreciation of the unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment in the submitted Inventory Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property so that the City could proceed in the disposal and immediately after the disposal, the City Accountant should draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop from the books the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO is still in the process of gathering records/documents, thus the reconciliation of both records was not undertaken.
observations.reason: The disposal of unserviceable properties was not undertaken due to difficulty of retrieving old records/documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the CDRRM Council to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the management of the Fund.
observations.managementAction: The City was unable to incorporate the projects under a separate caption as required.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the City was unable to incorporate under the separate caption the unexpended prior years balances of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the General
Services Officer (GSO) to exert more effort to reconcile the difference of
P20,039,399.26 existing between the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to exert more
efforts to retrieve the records of the subject PPE to be able to compute the
depreciation expense and make the necessary adjustments in the books.
Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant provide yearly depreciation
for all depreciable properties in accordance with the afore-mentioned rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting Office to make
necessary adjustments on the entry made for the undisposed PPE. Henceforth,
draw JEV to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after disposal, in
accordance with the aforecited regulations.

We further recommended that the CGSO and the City Accounting Office reconcile
their records on unserviceable properties and thereafter, the former facilitate the
immediate disposal thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Development Council to
scrutinize the projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund and ensure
that they contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes as prescribed by the aforementioned Joint
Memorandum Circular.

We further recommended that the City Mayor and the City Development Council
ensure re-appropriation of savings from the implemented prior years’ projects to
other priority development projects for their optimum utilization to be able to
provide optimum benefits to the public or intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Coordinator to indicate in the 20% Development Plan the timelines for the
implementation of development PPAs and thereafter, the BAC align the timing of
procurement processes with the timelines set in the 20% Development Plan to be
able to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and Section 7 of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor and the CSB regularly monitor and evaluate
the balance of the SEF to determine the actual unexpended balance thereof and
ensure its inclusion in the SEF budget in the ensuing year to maximize its utilization
in order to benefit the schools and enable them to implement other learning
activities.

We further recommended that the CSB strictly adhere to the guidelines provided
under the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017, through the
formulation/preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1, a three-year
program indicating strategic prioritization policies and strictly adhere to the
guidelines provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular for the allocation of SEF
and the matrix for monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and
utilization of the fund. Also, prepare and submit the quarterly and annual reports
to the concerned offices.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the CDRRM Council to identify and
incorporate under a separate caption in the CDRRMFIP the projects and activities
to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to facilitate
monitoring and evaluation of the management of fund, and prepare and submit to
the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We also recommended that the City Accountant be directed to revert back to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended balances of the
CDRRMF in CYs 2012 and 2013 to make them available for other social services of
the City after enactment by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC and its TWG to conduct a
thorough post-qualification of bidders and evaluation of bids. Henceforth,
disqualify contractors with simultaneous projects schedule and whose key
personnel and equipment were already pledged/used in their other ongoing
projects and direct the City Engineering Office to strictly monitor the still ongoing
simultaneous projects by the same contractor to avoid unwarranted delays or
substandard quality of work.

We further recommended that the City Engineering Office be required to furnish the
Office of the Auditor copies of the Suspension Orders with supporting documents,
issued for evaluation of the reasonableness/propriety of issuing the same and
thereafter impose liquidated damages on the contractor if the Suspension Order/s
issued will be found to be not acceptable.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit copies
of all Contracts within five working days from their perfection/execution to the
Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We further recommended that the concerned City officials prepare all contracts in
standard form prescribed under RIRR of RA No. 9184 by indicating and specifying
all relevant dates, the obligations of the contracting parties and the sanctions for
non-implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office to strictly
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by indicating on the
displayed project signboards all relevant information relative to the implemented
PPAs to fully inform the public and to effect full disclosure policy of the transactions
going public interest. Further, require such Office to submit at the beginning of the
year a list of ongoing and to be implemented PPAs for the year and to inform COA
within ten days after the award of the projects or before the start of the PPAs that
the appropriate project signboards are already posted on the project sites.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to require the
submission of an irrevocable standby Letter of Credit, bank guarantee, or surety
bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed
by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the City for the advanced amount
to the concerned contractor and henceforth, see to it that claims for
mobilization/advance payment are supported with all the required documents
prior to the payment thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the City’s GAD Focal Person and
the TWG are adequately trained to conduct gender analysis using HGDG
assessment/scoring tool in order that the PPAs attributed in the GAD Budget are
gender-sensitive and responsive by requiring them to attend GAD-related
Trainings/Seminars.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to exert more effort to reconcile the difference between the accounting records of PPE and Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). We also recommended that the City Mayor monitored the cause the close monitoring of the reconciliation between the City Accounting and the City GSO PPE records until the PPE account balance is fully reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor had monitored the cause the close monitoring of the reconciliation between the City Accounting and the City GSO PPE records.
observations.reason: Due to manpower constraints and the difficulty of retrieving records/documents, the reconciliation of both records was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor enjoin the City Accountant to: • exert effort to determine the details of the Land account and to prepare and maintain Real Property Ledger Card to facilitate the reconciliation of records maintained by the City General Services Office; and • coordinate with the General Services Officer and City Assessor to come up with the fair value of all the Land owned by the City to facilitate its recording in the books for fair presentation of the Land account.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had recorded in the books the parcels of land identified by the City Assessor.
observations.reason: The preparation of Real Property Ledger Cards is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Accountant to take up in the books the distribution of the Zoo Animals and reconcile the accounts, and drop from the books the Zoo Animals transferred to Albay Parks and Wildlife and adjust the balance of the account Due to LGU - Equity using the Prior Period Adjustment account by account.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had dropped from the books the Zoo Animals transferred to Albay Parks and Wildlife and adjusted the balance of the account Due to LGU - Equity using the Prior Period Adjustment account by account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Agriculturist to immediately coordinate with the officials of the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources for the possible transfer of the project to Corregidor Island Zones that is under the jurisdiction of Cavite City. We recommended that henceforth, the City carefully evaluate the feasibility of the projects to be requested for funding from the National Government to ensure their practicability and unhampered implementation that will lead to the attainment of the corresponding program objectives.
observations.managementAction: The City Agriculturist had already requested approval from the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources.
observations.reason: The project remained unutilized pending approval from the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure the use of the 20% Development Fund for the achievement of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the local government unit. We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Budget Officer to stop obligating expenditure items which are not related and/or connected with the programs/projects of the 20% Development Fund and the City Accountant to certify only to the availability of budgetary allotments to which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.
observations.managementAction: The City Budget Officer had obligated expenditures that were related to the implementation of the projects of the 20% Development Fund prescribed in the mentioned Joint Memorandum Circular. The City Accountant had certified expenditures with available budgetary allotment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts for the preceding calendar year or quarter; and certified photocopy of the Income Tax Returns (ITR) to be submitted on or before April 30 of each year to determine if a deficiency in business tax collected exists arising out of the difference in gross sales or receipts as declared in the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts and in the ITR; b. In-charge of Office (ICO) - City Treasurer or his duly authorized representative/s to examine the books of accounts and other pertinent records of the business establishments doing business within the City to ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of the tax due from the taxpayer concerned; c. City Accountant to submit to the ICO - City Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; d. ICO - City Treasurer to verify/assess the gross sales or receipts of the three subject sample contractors/suppliers, validate herein computed under collection totaling ₱63,763.53 and enforce the collection of underpayments including penalties and surcharges, if applicable; and e. ICO - City Treasurer to properly classify and bill taxpayers their business tax in accordance with Section 67 of the City’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Business Permits and Licensing Office had required the submission of the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts and the certified photo copy of the ITR from the owner of the business establishments. The ICO-City Treasurer already started examining the books of accounts and other pertinent records of the business establishments doing business within the City to ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of the tax due from the taxpayer concerned. The City furnished the ICO-City Treasurer the list of contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City. Letters had been sent to the suppliers to enforce collections of the business tax re-classification.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, we recommended that the City Accountant ensure complete and prompt submission to the Office of the Auditor of all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: All DVs were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a) ICO - City Treasurer to cancel the stale checks which are still unclaimed and, in her custody, and to send written notices to the payees; b) ICO - City Treasurer to conduct a regular monitoring and review on the status of checks issued which remains outstanding for a relatively long period of time and to send written notice to the payee of the existence of the check due to them at least one month before it becomes stale; c) City Accountant to strictly observe the specific policies and procedures in accounting for stale checks; and d) City Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take up in the books the reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-City Treasurer cancelled the stale checks and issued written notices to the payees. The ICO-City Treasurer had conducted regular monitoring and review on the status of all issued checks. The City Accountant strictly observed the specific policies and procedures in the accounting of stale checks. The City Accountant had already taken up in the books the reconciling items noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the following corrective measures:
• The City Accountant and the General Services Officer should exert more effort to reconcile the difference of P35,921,924.37 pertaining to PPE, the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE;
• The General Services Office should conduct physical inventory of assets taken up in the Special Education Fund books with acquisition cost of P2,397,011.59 to determine its actual existence and condition and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; and
• The Accounting Office should provide the data on Accumulated Depreciation of the unserviceable property in the IIRUP; and
• The City Mayor should create Appraisal and Disposal Committee to enable the proper disposal of the reported unserviceable property.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer coordinated with the Supply Officer of the different schools and conducted physical count of PPE. Corresponding RPCPPE was submitted to the Auditor. The Accounting Office could not provide data on Accumulated Depreciation of the unserviceable PPE due to absence of PPE Ledger Cards. The City Mayor had already issued an Executive Order No. 29, Series of 2017, an Order Reconstituting the member of the Joint Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee for serviceable and unserviceable, performing and non-performing assets of the City Government of Cavite and for other purpose.
observations.reason: Due to manpower constraints and inadequate records

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the City
Mayor direct the City Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) exert more effort to reconcile the
difference of P62,001,823.21 existing between the accounting records of PPE and
RPCPPE.

We also recommended that the City Mayor cause the close monitoring of the
reconciliation between the records of the City Accountant and the GSO until the PPE
account balance is fully reconciled.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor enjoined the City
Accountant to:
• exert effort to determine the details of the Land account and to
prepare and maintain Real Property Ledger Card to facilitate the
reconciliation of records maintained by the Accounting Office with
those of the General Services Office; and
• coordinate with the General Services Officer and City Assessor to
come up with the fair value of all the Land owned by the City
Government to facilitate its recording in the books for fair
presentation of the Land account.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Accountant to
immediately take up in the books the distribution of welfare goods and reconcile the
balances of Office Supplies Inventory and Accountable Forms Inventory accounts
with the Inventory Reports and records maintained by the City General Services
Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Accountant to
immediately drop from the books the value of the Zoo Animals transferred to Albay
Parks and Wildlife and to adjust the balance of the Due to LGUs – Albay account of
P551,900.00 to Equity using the Prior Period Adjustment account.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to provide
the Accumulated Depreciation of the unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment
in the submitted IIRUP so that the City could proceed in the disposal of these items.
Immediately after the disposal, the City Accountant should draw Journal Entry
Voucher to drop from the books the unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Agriculturist to
immediately coordinate with the officials of the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic
Resources for the possible transfer of the project to Corregidor Island Zones that is
under the jurisdiction of Cavite City.

We recommended that henceforth, the City Government carefully evaluate the
feasibility of the projects to be requested for funding from the National
Government to ensure its practicability and unhampered implementation that will
lead to the attainment of the corresponding program objectives.

We recommended that the City Mayor ensure the use of the 20% Development
Fund solely for the achievement of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes of the local government unit.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• City Budget Officer to stop obligating expenditure items which are not
related to and/or connected with the implementation of development
programs prescribed in the abovementioned Joint Memorandum
Circular against the 20% Development Fund; and
• City Accountant to certify only to the availability of budgetary
allotment to which expenditures and obligations may be properly
charged.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the LDRRM Council to identify
and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to facilitate
monitoring and evaluation of the management of the Fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit the
following documents when paying their business taxes:
• Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts for the preceding
calendar year or quarter; and
• certified photocopy of Income Tax Returns (ITR) to be
submitted on or before April 30 of each year to determine if a
deficiency in business tax collected exists arising out of the
difference in gross sales or receipts as declared in the Sworn
Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts and in the ITR.
b. In-charge of Office (ICO) - City Treasurer or his duly authorized
representative/s to examine the books of accounts and other pertinent
records of the business establishments doing business within the city to
ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of the tax due
from the taxpayer concerned;
c. City Accountant to submit to the ICO – City Treasurer a List of Payments
made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in
the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of
business taxes in the current year;
d. ICO – City Treasurer to verify/assess the gross sales or receipts of the three
subject sample contractors/suppliers, validate herein computed
undercollection totaling ₽63,763.52 and enforce the collection of
underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant
to the rates provided in the City’s Revenue Code. Absence of documented
action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the
issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge; and
e. ICO - City Treasurer to properly classify and bill taxpayers their business
tax in accordance with Section 67 of the City’s Revenue Code.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to locate
and submit immediately the 135 disbursement vouchers and its supporting
documents involving a total amount of P42,063,665.37. Otherwise, a Notice of
Suspension will be issued by the Audit Team in accordance with the pertinent
provision of the RRSA.

Henceforth, we recommended that the City Accountant ensure complete and
prompt submission to the Auditor of all disbursement vouchers and its supporting
documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month in compliance with
Section 7.2.1(a) of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a) ICO - City Treasurer to cancel the stale checks which are still unclaimed and
in her custody, and to send written notices to the payees in possession of such
checks so that appropriate remedial actions can be undertaken to replace the
said stale checks;
b) ICO – City Treasurer to conduct a regular monitoring and review on the status
of checks issued which remains outstanding for a relatively long period of time
and to send written notice to the payee of the existence of the check due to them
at least one month before it becomes stale;
c) City Accountant to strictly observe the specific policies and procedures in
accounting for stale checks; and
d) City Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take
up in the books the reconciling items. (Proposed Journal Entries attached as
Annex 10)

We also recommended that she exert effort to determine the details of the Land account and to prepare and maintain Real Property Ledger Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of records maintained by the Accounting Office with those of the General Services Office. Further, we recommended that she ensure that all the land owned by the City were taken up in the books for fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: As commented by the City Accountant, their office has no details of the Land account taken up in the books, thus they cannot prepare the RPLC. However, she promised to coordinate with the Office of the Assessor to determine which parcels of land should be recorded in the books. Moreover, the City Assessor has already furnished the Offices of the Auditor, Accounting and General Services with the List of Assessments of Real Properties Owned by the City Government with the correct values that could be used as the starting point in recording of Land in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Absence of records pertaining to its acquisition

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments in the Accumulated Depreciation of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment of the City Government and in the Government Equity. Henceforth, use the revised residual value which is 5% of the cost, in line with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher No. 2017 - 3275G dated June 30, 2017 was drawn by the Accounting Office to record the adjustments in the Accumulated Depreciation of the PPE. Henceforth, use the revised residual value which is 5% of the cost, in line with PPSAS 17.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant undertake the following:
a. reconcile the Real Property Tax Accounting Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables with the Records of Real Property Tax Delinquencies maintained by the City Treasurer’s Office;
b. coordinate with the City Treasurer’s Office to obtain the desired information on the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables such as the name of taxpayers, amount and period of delinquencies, and other related information; and
c. maintain and update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer’s Office reconciled the balances of SET and RPT Receivables with the records maintained by the City Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO – City Treasurer draw cash advances for Payroll Fund equal to the net amount of payroll for a period and to be consistent whether the payment of Salaries shall be on a weekly or semi-monthly basis. We also recommended that the City Accountant require that the payroll be attached as supporting document to the disbursement vouchers for cash advances for payment of salaries, wages and other benefits. Further, we recommended that management consider the option of paying salaries and allowances of municipal employees through Automatic Teller Machine of the government depository bank.
observations.managementAction: Management had been able to reduce the amount of excess withdrawal of cash advances for payroll. Salaries were made on semi-monthly basis. Payrolls were now attached to the disbursement vouchers and other personnel benefits. The City Government had coordinated with Land Bank of the Philippines and starting January 2018, salaries and allowances of regular employees were paid through ATM.
observations.reason: Some City Departments hindered the full implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned department heads of the City, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serve as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process. We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the form prescribed in the LGU Procurement Manual, indicating therein, among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: PPMP were prepared in 2017 for the procurement of infrastructure projects. Procurement of infrastructure projects was in accordance with the timeline prescribed under RA 9184. Annual Procurement Plan was likewise being prepared by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the balance of Office Supplies Inventory with the records of the General Services Office and to prepare and maintain the Stock Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock to come up with a reliable balance of Office Supplies Inventory account.
observations.managementAction: The City GSO and Accounting Office coordinated and the physical counts were conducted. The Accounting Office relied on the records being maintained by the City Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: Absence of stock ledger card to be prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office hindered the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant verify outstanding balances of Accounts Payable against which no actual claims, administrative or judicial, have been filed or are not covered with perfected contracts on record and those found to be without valid claim or undocumented, irrespective of age, and thereby, cause their reversion to the unappropriated surplus for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office verified the balances of Accounts Payables outstanding for two years or more and reverted those found to be without valid claim.

The City had created GAD Focal Point system thru an executive order No. 7, Series of 2017 issued last March 3, 2017 and is now functional and taking the lead in spearheading the GAD Programs, Projects and Activities. The GAD Focal Point had identified the gender issues and concerns to be provided with funding allocations. The City was able to come up with a comprehensive GAD Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report. Utilization of the allocations for GAD was in accordance with Budget ad Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. ICO - City Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/ receipts (not only from the City Government of Cavite) for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent records/ information for the determination of gross receipts; and
b. City Accountant to submit to the ICO - City Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.
observations.managementAction: Letters to the concerned contractors/suppliers had been sent by the ICO - City Treasurer informing them of the observation regarding the uncollected business taxes. They also requested for the submission of financial statements, receipts and other pertinent records for the determination of the gross receipts and for them to settle the difference the soonest possible time.
observations.reason: Submission of sworn statements from all contractors/suppliers is yet to be required by the BPLO. The City Treasurer’s Office enforced the collection of P93,350.72.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City Officials to observe the proper preparation of the Statement of Itemized Peace and Order Program (POP) and budgetary limitations on the appropriation for Confidential Fund as provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Statement of Itemized Peace and Order Program (POP) and budgetary limitations on the appropriation for Confidential Fund was now being prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor expedite the use of the P1,200,000.00 for the implementation of the fishery projects to generate employment and alleviate the lives of the marginalized fishermen/fish farmers of the City, otherwise, return the amount to BFAR Region IV-A as provided in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: In adherence to the audit recommendation, the City Agricultural Officer sent a letter dated April 11, 2018 to the BFAR and Provincial Fishery Officer requesting approval of the transfer of location of the project. As stated in the letter reply dated April 18, 2018 of the OIC Regional Director of BFAR, said office agreed to the revision of project location provided that the conditions set forth in the guidelines are met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to require Wismon Construction and Development Corporation to pay the maximum ceiling of liquidated damages of P9,860,940.59 for failure to complete the project within the approved extension period in accordance with Section 68 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 or deduct the maximum ceiling of liquidated damages from the other claims of the contractor with the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Legal proceedings were being undertaken by the City to collect the liquidated damages from the Contractor.
observations.reason: Legal Proceedings were being undertaken by the City to collect the liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the General Services Officer and City Assessor to come up with the fair value of all the Land owned by the City Government.  We also recommended that she exert effort to determine the details of the Land account and to prepare and maintain Real Property Ledger Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of records maintained by the Accounting Office with those of the General Services Office.  Further, we recommended that she ensure that all the land owned by the City were taken up in the books for fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments in the Accumulated Depreciation of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment of the City Government and in Government Equity. Henceforth, use the revised residual value which is 5% of the cost, in line with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17.

We recommended that ICO – City Treasurer draw cash advances for Payroll Fund equal to the net amount of payroll for a pay period and to be consistent whether the payment of salaries shall be on a weekly or semi-monthly basis.
We also recommended that the City Accountant require that the payroll be attached as supporting document to the disbursement vouchers for cash advances for payment of salaries, wages, allowances and other emoluments to ascertain that the amount to be granted tally with the total net pay.
Further, we recommended that management consider the option of paying salaries and allowances of municipal employees through Automatic Teller Machine of the government depository bank.

We recommended that the concerned department heads of the City, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serve as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process.
We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the form prescribed in the LGU Procurement Manual, indicating therein, among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities. Moreover, schedule the bidding of infrastructure projects when funds are already available and within the timeframe provided in RA No. 9184 and the proximity of the project implementation.
We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC Chairman to see to it that the required timeline for the issuance of Notice to Proceed and contract signing after the Notice of Award are properly monitored and complied with in accordance with the regulations of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the RA No. 9184.  Likewise address the delays in the procurement process.

We recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the balance of Office Supplies Inventory with the records of the General Services Office and to prepare and maintain Stock Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock to come up with a reliable balance of Office Supplies Inventory account.

We recommended that the City Accountant verify balances of Accounts Payable outstanding for two years or more and againstwhich no actual claims, administrative or judicial, have been filed or are not covered with perfected contracts on record and those found to be without valid claim or undocumented, irrespective of age, and cause their reversion to the unappropriated surplus for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
ICO - City Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/ receipts (not only from the City Government of Cavite) for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent records/ information for the determination of gross receipts.
City Accountant to submit to the ICO - City Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.
ICO – City Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling ₽93,350.72, and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the City’s Revised Revenue Code. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

•	City Accountant and General Services Officer to prioritize the immediate reconciliation of their records pertaining to Land. JEV shall be drawn to take up reconciling items; and

•	Accounting Unit to maintain Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLCs) and reconcile the balances therein with the property records kept by the General Services Office.
observations.managementAction: The General Services was able to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for Land.
observations.reason: Unimplemented | Lack of adequate records pertaining to Land account in the Accounting Office hinders the reconciliation of records with that of the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the  City Mayor direct the:

•	City Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office to prioritize the complete reconciliation of  the balances of the PPE accounts to make certain the reliability, existence and correctness of the  Property, Plant and Equipment; and

•	Office of the City Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards while the General Service Office should maintain Property Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property balances.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of balances of PPE between the Accounting and GSO records were still ongoing and the discrepancy was reduced to P35,921,924.37 as of year-end.



The GSO prepares and maintains Property Cards while the Accounting Office, due to manpower constraints, has yet to come up with updated PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Due to manpower constraints and inadequate records pertaining to  PPE, the reconciliation of records between the Accounting and General Services Offices was not fully  undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the reported RPT/SET Receivables as of  year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office coordinated with the City Treasurer and has drawn Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the balances of RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Absence of subsidiary records pertaining to RPT/SET Receivables accounts in the Accounting Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CSWDO to submit the inventory of the Welfare Goods for Distribution to the Accounting Office for reconciliation and adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The City Social Worker and Development Office submitted the Inventory of Welfare Goods to the Accounting Office.  Request and Issuance Slip, together with the List of Recipients, was forwarded by the GSO to the Accounting Office for taking up in the books the distribution of the inventory items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City Officials to observe the proper preparation of the Statement of Itemized Peace and Order Program (POP) and budgetary limitations on the appropriation for Confidential Fund as provided under Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 issued by  COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines in the preparation of the POP were rolled out and the LGU was able to come up with the 2017-2019 Plan which is in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2015-1.
observations.reason: The concerned department heads and their staff attended the training on POP planning and formulation in December 2016 only.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

•	City Accountant to require the City officials/employees concerned to submit the liquidation vouchers and their supporting documents to the Accounting Office to support the liquidation of the cash advances totaling P411,725.00; and

•	officials/employees concerned to refund the excess traveling allowance totaling P18,720.00 and henceforth, follow the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 in claiming said allowance  to avoid disallowance  in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents were submitted to the Accounting Office.



The amounts representing excess travelling allowance were refunded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local School Board to:

•	refrain from charging to SEF the expenses for the honoraria of Asatidz under the Madrasah Education Program (MEP), honoraria of teachers under the Alternative Learning System (ALS) and food expenses in the celebration of Teacher’s Day; and

•	see to it that SEF is utilized for purposes provided under Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, as implemented by DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-B dated June 25, 2001, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01 dated April 14, 1998 to ensure that the purposes for which the fund was established are met and consider only the programs/projects enumerated thereat in the preparation of the SEF budget.
observations.managementAction:
The granting of honoraria to Asatids was discontinued in 2016 while that of ALS was justified by the City Division Office because the Mobile volunteer teachers were not receiving any compensation from the national government.  


Guidelines in the utilization of SEF were being followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of     Budget and     Management  (DBM)    Joint    Memorandum   Circular     No.   2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to ensure that the purpose for which it was established is met.
observations.managementAction: The LGU justified and submitted documents that will support/prove that the projects implemented under the 20% Development Funds were within the guidelines.  There was an oversight on the use of technical jargon in the project titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor submit to the Audit Team a resolution or ordinance enacted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod ratifying the release and use of  P5,980,000.00 to purchase 2,600 cavans of rice chargeable against the 30% QRF of the City. Henceforth, adherence to paragraph 6.6.3 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 must be observed for future transactions of the same nature.
observations.managementAction: The City Government adopted the Provincial Government Declaration of Cavite under the State of Calamity.  The same had been stated in the CDRRM Resolutions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

•	request from the National Housing Authority for an extension of time in the implementation of the Project; and

•	direct the concerned City officials to follow strictly the provisions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) in carrying out the Project. Any deviations in the project implementations must have authority from the NHA.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU requested for an extension of time in the implementation of the project and the same was approved by the NHA.

The provisions stipulated in the MOA were followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor expedite the use of the P1,200,000.00 for the implementation of the fishery projects to generate employment and alleviate the lives of  the 1,955 marginalized fishermen/fish farmers of the City, otherwise, return the amount to BFAR Region IV-A as provided for in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Out of the total P1.2 million, P600,000.00 was expended for the purchase of motorized banca and fishing nets
observations.reason: The remaining P600,000.00 was to be refunded because the creation of fish sanctuary project was not feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to remit to the National Treasury the amount of P500,000.00 sourced from PDAF credited in the account of the City without corresponding document/purpose to comply with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P500,000.00 was remitted to the National Treasury in April 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

•	direct the City Accountant to require Wismon Construction and Development Corporation to pay the maximum ceiling of liquidated damages of P9,860,940.59 for failure to complete the project within the approved extension period in accordance with Section 68 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 or deduct the same from the other claims of the contractor with the City Government; and

•	implement the necessary measures/remedies to complete the Construction of a New Four (4) Storey City Hall Building at Paseo de Barangay, San Roque, Cavite City before the structures that have been constructed deteriorate. The City Mayor may rescind the contract, forfeit the contractor’s performance security and may take over the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor as provided for in Item 8.4, Annex E of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Final Notice of Demand for the payment of the liquidated damages was issued to the Contractor by the City Mayor.  



The construction of the new city hall building was bid out on December 2, 2016 and awarded to a qualified contractor.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented | The City is still awaiting payment of the liquidated damages from Wismon Construction and Development Corporation.
